We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Claims Analyst
description: We are looking for an experienced Claims Analyst to join our growing business. This is a fast\-paced role in a high\-volume environment, supporting a large patient base across dispensing and claims. The ideal candidate will have strong knowledge of Irish pharmacy schemes, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to work accurately under strict deadlines. This role is suited to someone who is highly organised, a strong communicator, and a team player who can work closely with multiple departments while supporting continuous improvement across the business. **Key Responsibilities** * Keep claims verification processes accurate, up to date, and in line with current requirements * Review, analyse, and manage claims in a high\-volume setting * Liaise with the accounts and billing team to support billing\-related queries and processes * Support bills and customer service functions as required * Work closely with the Training Lead to identify gaps, trends, and opportunities for improvement * Communicate effectively with the pharmacy technician team to provide guidance and support * Analyse claim rejections thoroughly and take the appropriate steps to reclaim where possible * Maintain a comprehensive understanding of Irish pharmacy schemes and claims processes * Ensure compliance with relevant legislation, HSE, and PCRS requirements * Prioritise workload effectively and meet strict deadlines consistently **Requirements** * Previous experience in a claims analyst role or similar pharmacy claims position * Strong working knowledge of Irish pharmacy schemes * Excellent understanding of relevant legislation, HSE, and PCRS processes * Experience dealing with claims verification, rejects analysis, and reclaim processes * Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy * Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Team player with a proactive and solution\-focused approach

title: Clinical Specialist Radiographer MRI
description: Applications are currently open for the role of Clinical Specialist Radiographer in St. Vincent's Private Hospital **Essential Criteria** * Have the Diploma of the College of Radiographers (Diagnostic) or have a Bachelor of Science or its equivalent in Diagnostic Radiography. * Qualification must be recognised and validated by the Irish Institute of Radiography. * Have a minimum of 6 years post graduate experience in Radiography. * Be CORU registered. * Have a post graduate qualification in MRI plus a minimum of three years post MRI qualification experience in MRI. * Demonstrate experience of working with a team of Radiographers. * Have proven ability to work under pressure. **Desirable Criteria** * Demonstrate ability to manage clinical and non\-clinical situations. * Have excellent interpersonal and communication skills. * Demonstrate evidence of continuing professional development. * Have a high capacity for responsibility and individual initiative. A full Job description is available on request, from the HR Office\- hr@svph.ie Interested candidates should apply by sending an up\-to\-date CV by email to hr@svph.ie Closing Date: Monday 7th April 2026

title: Clinical Specialist Sonographer (Paediatrics & DDH)
description: **Job Description** **Position Summary:** The Paediatric Clinical Specialist Sonographer will deliver a high\-quality, child\-centred ultrasound service across Medica Diagnostics Ireland sites. Working autonomously within Medica’s clinical governance framework and agreed protocols, the post holder will independently undertake a wide range of paediatric ultrasound examinations and produce accurate, comprehensive provisional ultrasound reports to support timely clinical decision\-making. The role carries specialist responsibility for maintaining excellence in paediatric imaging practice, including referral vetting, oversight of ultrasound equipment performance, and the consistent application of quality assurance measures. The post holder will contribute to protocol development and review, clinical audit, service evaluation, and continuous improvement activity, while acting as a key point of escalation for complex clinical, operational, or equipment\-related issues within the paediatric ultrasound service. As a senior clinical practitioner, the post holder will also play a central role in education, training, and mentorship. This includes supporting the development of trainee and junior sonographers, student radiographers, and wider imaging colleagues through formal teaching, supervision, and day\-to\-day professional guidance. They will help foster a positive learning culture that promotes clinical excellence, professional growth, and safe paediatric practice. The successful candidate will demonstrate flexibility and leadership in supporting service development, cross\-site working, and future expansion of paediatric ultrasound provision, ensuring delivery remains aligned with Medica Diagnostics Ireland’s strategic and operational priorities. **Key Responsibilities** **Clinical Responsibilities** * To be responsible for independently performing a wide range of high\-quality paediatric diagnostic ultrasound examinations in accordance with Medica Diagnostics Ireland procedures, policies, and clinical protocols. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of clinical and technical developments in paediatric sonography, ensuring evidence\-based practice is reflected in scanning protocols and contributing to the development, review, and refinement of local examination techniques and service standards. * Vet ultrasound referrals to ensure clinical appropriateness and justification in line with professional, legislative, and local guidance. Clarify referral details where required and prioritise patients appropriately according to clinical urgency to support safe and timely care. * Apply advanced clinical judgement and specialist paediatric ultrasound expertise in image acquisition, interpretation, and reporting. Produce high\-quality preliminary ultrasound reports, as locally agreed, and escalate urgent, unexpected, or significant findings without delay to support safe patient management. * Maintain accurate, timely, and complete patient records using Medica’s RIS/PACS and associated clinical systems. Ensure all examinations are correctly annotated, documented, and archived in accordance with organisational standards, professional requirements, and data protection legislation. * Adhere to all Medica Diagnostics Ireland policies and procedures relevant to clinical practice, including safeguarding, infection prevention and control, consent, chaperoning, health and safety, clinical record\-keeping, and communication protocols. * Practise within the limits of personal competence and scope of practice. Seek advice, second opinions, or escalate appropriately to the Head of Ultrasound Services, senior clinical colleagues, or reporting radiologists where clinical complexity, uncertainty, or risk requires. * Carry out daily equipment quality checks and basic troubleshooting of ultrasound systems, ensuring equipment remains safe and fit for purpose. Promptly report faults or malfunctions to the Head of Ultrasound Services and vendor support services and communicate any potential service impact to relevant operational leads. * Ensure a child\-centred, compassionate, and professional approach, adapting communication and examination techniques to meet the needs of infants, children, young people, and their families or carers. * Act as a clinical specialist resource within paediatric ultrasound, supporting safe decision\-making, consistent practice, and high standards of care across the service. **Quality, Education and Training** * Contribute to Medica Diagnostics Ireland’s clinical audit and quality assurance activities, including peer review, image quality checks, and documentation review, supporting high standards in paediatric ultrasound practice. * Support the development and review of paediatric ultrasound protocols, quality control processes, and documentation standards to promote consistency, safety, and best practice across the service. * Participate in clinical governance activities, including audit feedback, incident review, and service improvement initiatives, helping to promote a culture of learning, accountability, and continuous improvement. * Report incidents, near misses, and patient safety concerns promptly through the appropriate reporting systems, supporting effective risk management and safe service delivery. * Provide day\-to\-day guidance and support to trainee and junior sonographers and other relevant staff, contributing to their development and helping to foster a positive learning environment. * Contribute to shared learning through case discussion, reflective practice, and participation in internal education and service development activities. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge and skills in paediatric ultrasound through continuing professional development, mandatory training, and participation in relevant internal and external learning opportunities. * Promote clinical excellence, professional development, and high\-quality child\-centred care across the paediatric ultrasound service. **Professional Conduct And Interpersonal Responsibilities** * Maintain effective, respectful communication with colleagues, radiologists, administrative teams, and non\-radiographic staff across all clinical sites to ensure seamless service delivery. * Adhere to Medica’s Communication Policy and guidelines for operational updates, issue reporting, and MDT coordination. Ensure timely and accurate information flow between sonographers, radiologists, referrers, and clinic staff. * Escalate any issues related to the roster or scheduling to Media Operations manager. * Report complaints or service concerns through the appropriate quality management system, ensuring the Head of Ultrasound is informed in a timely manner. * Represent and promote the Medica brand within clinics through professional behaviour, a collaborative attitude, and a commitment to excellence. * Uphold the highest standards of professionalism by treating every patient with courtesy, empathy, efficiency, and confidentiality, delivering a safe, respectful, and patient\-centred service at all times. **Job Requirements** **Qualifications:** * BSc Diagnostics Radiography * MSc in Medical Ultrasound or PgD in Medical Ultrasound * CORU registered **Essential Skills** * 3 years’ experience in acute paediatric radiology setting * Communication * Patient care * Technical Competency * Attention to detail * Teamwork * Time management **Desirable Skills** * Problem solving * Adaptability * Mentorship and leadership * Audit and Research

title: Commissioning Engineer
description: **About the Position** We have an exciting opportunity for a Commissioning Engineer, to join STS Group T\&D Division. The position will be located in Ireland. STS Group, is dedicated to being the leading and most cost\-efficient provider of electrical engineering, instrumentation, design and commissioning services across Ireland, UK, Europe and the Middle East. Since its formation, STS Group has broadened its scope to become a full\-service provider to world renowned brand names in the Pharmaceutical, Data Centre, Utilities, Oil, Gas \& Chemicals, Food \& Beverage, Transport and other industry sectors. Headquartered in Waterford, Ireland, STS Group is one of three business units within Dussmann Technical Solutions (DTS) and is a proud member of the Dussmann Group. STS Group employs the best people, invests heavily in training and education ensuring that the highest standards of health, safety and governance are applied throughout the organisation **What you will be doing?** * Compile commissioning packs for upcoming projects. * Attend project commissioning kick off and co\-ordination meetings. * Drive the commissioning program. * Completes project RFI/TQ’s during commissioning and manage the design log process. * AttendFAT \& SAT tests when requested / Required. * Ensures all required site modifications are completed and validate as\-built drawings. * Completion of commissioning and test documentation throughout project lifespan. * Configuration of protection relays, RTU, PLC, Power quality meters, and other substation automation systems * Assist configuration and testing of SCADA systems. * Completion of pre and post energisation testing. * Commissioning of primary and secondary equipment on projects. * Completion and submission of redline drawings during commissioning. * Provide declaration of fitness when commissioning is complete. * Assists junior commissioning engineers with their development. **Is this the job for me?** * Trade qualification through Apprenticeship or Engineering qualification. * 5 years’ \+ relevant industry experience. * Flexibility/Adaptability. * IT Literacy. * Strong communication skills. * Operational experience with test equipment. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary. * Educational Assistance, CPD and upskilling opportunities Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects. * STS Group are an equal opportunities employer.

title: Company Secretary Manager
description: An established international financial services organisation within the funds industry is seeking to appoint a Senior Manager – Company Secretarial to support a growing portfolio of Irish regulated entities and fund structures. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced company secretarial professional to step into a leadership role, managing client relationships, overseeing board governance processes and supporting the development of a high\-performing team. The successful candidate will manage a portfolio of Irish fund entities while leading and mentoring members of the company secretarial team. The role involves regular interaction with directors, clients and key stakeholders to ensure high standards of governance and regulatory compliance. **Key Responsbilities** * Managing a portfolio of Irish entities including preparation of board packs, agendas and minutes for board and committee meetings. * Acting as a key point of contact for directors, clients and internal stakeholders. * Ensuring timely follow\-up on matters arising from board meetings and maintaining strong communication with clients. * Ensuring compliance with applicable corporate governance and company law requirements. * Managing regulatory filings and statutory obligations for Irish entities. * Monitoring developments in corporate governance and company law and advising boards on relevant obligations. * Leading and mentoring junior team members and supporting the development of the company secretarial function. * Reviewing and improving operational procedures and internal governance processes. * Monitoring regulatory developments affecting the funds industry and assessing their impact on the function. **Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in law, business, accounting or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years’ experience in a company secretarial role within financial services, ideally within the funds industry. * Strong knowledge of Irish funds regulation and corporate governance requirements. * Demonstrated experience in team leadership or people management. * Experience supporting boards and maintaining relationships with senior stakeholders. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Compliance Analyst
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way Compliance Analyst (12 Months Fixed Term Contract) Technology at Primark Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Pension,27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Do as a** **Compliance Analyst** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Develop a strong understanding of the current system and application landscape to support future solution design. * Assess practices and policies against Primark’s compliance and security standards, identifying and prioritising any gaps with vendors and product teams. * Collaborate with security teams, vendors, and developers to initiate, analyse, and action outcomes from vulnerability scans. * Represent security, compliance, and regulatory needs within project requirements and ensure they are championed across product teams. * Manage compliance activities, including cookie compliance and security scanning across the digital landscape, ensuring timely remediation and risk mitigation. * Provide regular reporting on compliance and security risks while supporting multiple initiatives in parallel. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Strong experience with digital cookie consent management tools, online security, and compliance initiatives. * Ability to analyse vulnerability scans and translate findings into actionable remediation steps. * Solid functional and technical understanding of digital systems, including websites, platforms, and related operations. * Excellent communication skills with the ability to build strong internal and external relationships, including with senior stakeholders. * Strong planning and organisational skills, with the ability to manage workloads, prioritise tasks, and meet agreed timelines. * Knowledge of security and compliance requirements, supported by relevant education (degree or equivalent). Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-5745

title: Counter Associate "The Ordinary" Arnotts
description: **WHAT ON EARTH IS DECIEM?** We are a collective of disrupters and doers, of creatives and scientists, with a mission to reimagine the world of beauty. Since inception, our vertically integrated structure, including our own laboratories, manufacturing, retail stores, and brand infrastructure, have enabled us to rapidly identify opportunities and deliver quality. Home to The Ordinary, NIOD, AVESTAN, and other emerging brands, our goal is to create with purpose. We were founded on the principle of doing everything that others don’t. Because normal gets you nowhere. We are DECIEM, The Abnormal Beauty Company. We are growing rapidly and we’re looking for someone who shares this vision and wants to grow with us! **WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR** We are looking for a flexible customer service\-oriented Sales Assistant known at DECIEM as Counter Associate, to join our " **extraOrdinary"** team in **Arnotts, Dublin** to deliver memorable experiences to our customers \<3 Experience in the **Retail, Department Stores, and luxury industry** is highly desirable and ideally, within the **Fragrance, Beauty, Fashion** , and Luxury Goods sectors. * This is a 24\-hour\-a\-week position on a part\-time basis **WHAT YOU'D BE DOING IN THIS ROLE** * Greet all customers who enter the store with enthusiasm and professionalism reflecting DECIEM’s vision through excellent service * Determine the customer’s needs and suggest relevant products by listening to the customer and sharing the product knowledge acquired during brand training * Ensure standards for quality, customer service, and health and safety are met * Adhere to the DECIEM experience and deliver unparalleled customer service. Educate customers about the world of Beauty and Skin Care, DECIEM brands, and products * Manage customer feedback and queries * Assist in maintaining counter standards of cleanliness and organization * Be aware of and assist in maintaining DECIEM’s merchandising concepts * Ensure tidiness standards are always respected and maintained * Conduct product refunds and address customer issues in a positive manner * Responsible for building meaningful relationships with department managers and clients. High standard communication. **SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS NEEDED TO GET THE JOB DONE** - Comfortable in engaging customers and applying products to the customer while providing consultations - Ability to multi\-task, while providing professional and outstanding customer service Desire to spread brand awareness - Effective time management and communication skills. **WHAT YOU'LL BE GAINING BY JOINING OUR TEAM** There’s a lot of good stuff that comes along with being a DECIEM team member. Here’s a few of our favourite perks and benefits, in no particular order… Generous Vacation \& Personal Days, plus additional time off for volunteering in your community, voting, peaceful protesting, celebrating your birthday, and more. 6 months of paid time off for new parents (inclusive of all genders). Unlimited access to an Employee Assistance Program that includes mental health care, mindfulness programs, and more. Access to Development Grants \& a LinkedIn Learning membership to help you keep growing and learning. First dibs on new products. A generous discount on DECIEM products for you, your family, and your friends. **BUILDING GROWTH TO POWER GOOD** We are a people\-first company that lives by our core values to… * ✨ Defy Convention. * ✨ Make it real. * ✨ Play in the grey. * ✨ Build Bridges. * ✨ Be the rabbit. These values continue to guide us in all that we do. Along the way, we’ve been honoured to win some pretty incredible awards, including a CEW Achiever Award, Best Skincare Brand at Sunday Times Style Beauty Awards, and Most Popular Sustainable Brand by The Cosmetify Index Global. We’re still blushing! **SUSTAINABILITY AND SOCIAL IMPACT** DECIEM’s objective is to build growth and do good things while prioritizing people, animals, and the Earth. Our Sustainability strategy is focused on fighting climate change, improved waste management, responsible packaging, responsible sourcing and product lifecycle, and water conservation. In 2020, DECIEM established the Good Fund to support both large and grassroots charities in response to global events. **CREATING A HUMAN WORLD OF BEAUTY** DECIEM is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) by creating meaningful, measurable change in the lives of our team members and in the communities where we operate. Achieving equality is critical to DECIEM and our future. You can learn more about our DEI commitment in our open letter, " DECIEM is an equal opportunity employer. We prohibit discrimination based on age, colour, disability, national origin, race, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, and any other legally protected class in accordance with applicable federal, provincial and local laws. We are also committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible workplace. If you are contacted to be part of our recruitment process and require accommodation, please let us know. **WANT TO JOIN US?** Thank you for reading the whole thing! If you liked what you heard, kindly send us your resume. We really appreciate your effort in applying for this position. However, only those who have been shortlisted for interviews will be contacted. \<3 \#SP

title: Customer Success Manager
description: Our client is a leading company dedicated to connecting students with career opportunities. Their mission is to empower young people to find the right roles, gain confidence, and unlock their potential. They achieve this by collaborating with employers, universities, and other technology partners, providing innovative tools, content, and platforms that help students and graduates succeed in the professional world. The Role: Customer Success Manager The core of this role is to delight our clients by ensuring they meet their recruitment goals. You will be responsible for managing a portfolio of clients, nurturing those relationships, and providing exceptional service that leads to long\-term partnerships and subscription renewals. You'll be the voice of the client internally, providing valuable insights to help shape our product and service offerings. Key Responsibilities * **Client Management and Retention:** Serve as the main point of contact for a portfolio of clients. You will manage their accounts from onboarding to renewal, ensuring their subscriptions are optimized to meet their recruitment objectives. * **Strategic Advising:** Conduct regular check\-ins and review meetings to understand client needs, provide intelligent analysis of their performance data, and offer strategic recommendations. * **Customer Service Excellence:** Build strong, trusted relationships with clients through professional, timely, and charismatic communication. You will handle all administrative tasks related to subscriptions and seek out new ways to add value to their experience. * **Collaboration and Improvement:** Work with internal teams to communicate client needs and identify opportunities for upselling. You will also contribute to improving internal processes and workflows to boost team efficiency. Who You Are You are a highly organized, quick\-learning individual with a proactive and adaptable mindset. You can manage a large number of client relationships with ease and are a strong communicator, both written and verbally. You're comfortable with data analysis and can confidently explain insights to clients. You're a natural problem\-solver, always looking for ways to improve processes and deliver a better customer experience. Your Experience \& Skills * Experience in a customer\-facing or administrative role. * Familiarity with the student and graduate recruitment landscape is a plus. * Proficiency with Microsoft Office and CRM systems. * Excellent communication and time management skills. * A collaborative spirit and a drive to build strong relationships with colleagues and clients. Please click Apply Now to find out more!

title: Data Management Analyst
description: **Working Model: This is a hybrid role \- 50% remote/office based** **Closing Date: 02/04/2026** **About the Role:** Royal London Ireland is seeking applications for a **Data Management Analyst** to join our team in Dublin on a fixed term basis (6 to 9 months). As a Data Analyst, you’ll bring a natural curiosity to digging into data and uncovering what really matters. You won’t just pull reports — you’ll turn numbers into meaningful insights and clear commentary that helps your business partners make smarter decisions. You’ll explore new data sources, help connect and build them out, and translate everything into practical, actionable takeaways for the team. It’s a great role if you love learning: you’ll get the chance to develop new skills, try out new tools and software, and grow in a supportive, fast‑moving environment. **About The Role** * Full use of the MS Office Product Suite – especially high level Excel use * Ability to use SQL to manage and process data * Strong analytical foundation **About You** * Interpret data into a clear narrative * Ability to use own initiative * Good organisation and time management skills * Problem solving skills and attention to detail * Ability to work as part of a team and liaise with other areas of the business * Strong communication skills * Willingness to learn and develop * Good IT knowledge and experience, especially Microsoft Office \& similar business tools. * Strong numerical and technical skills * Strong attention to detail * Able to generate own ideas and pursue lines of thinking – a self\-starter **What We Offer** * Competitive employee benefits such as market leading Pension scheme (including Life Insurance); performance related bonus; health insurance allowance * Our culture comes from within, it comes from our people; our People Promise is our commitment to provide the best experience for our people. It reflects our brand, our strengths and how we work together to create a positive culture * On Glassdoor, Royal London Group is consistently ranked as among the best places to work * Based in our St Stephen’s Green office (Dublin 2\) with benefit from our hybrid work arrangements with 50:50 mix of office and home working.

title: Data analyst
description: Permanent role \| Data analyst \| Hybrid, Westmeath **Requirement** – 2\+ years experience with Python/ R – Strong knowledge of SQL and experience in data mining/ queries/ segmentation **– Financial Services/ Banking/ Insurance Experience Is Required.** EU/Irish citizenship or Dependent 1G/Stamp 4 can be considered. Apply Now

title: Dietitian - Nutrition Development Specialist
description: **About Abbott** Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life\-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals and branded generic medicines. Our 107,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. **Abbott Ireland Commercial Liffey Valley Dublin** Opened in 1946, this operation, based in Liffey Valley Dublin was the first Abbott business unit to be established in Ireland. This business is responsible for the sales, marketing and distribution of a wide range of diabetes, diagnostic, and nutritional products in Ireland **This is a 9\-month contract, full time job Monday to Friday, office based.** **Primary Function** The Nutrition Development Specialist will support Nutrition Services, marketing and regulatory team, through providing Dietetic support and medical back up for reimbursement submissions, for new campaigns, product launches and training. This role will be based in our Liffey Valley office reporting directly into the Commercial Lead. **Education / Experience \& Background** * Registered dietitian (with CORU) with 1\-2 years of clinical experience * Excellent knowledge of adult nutrition support – paediatric nutrition support * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * Excellent written and spoken English and communication skills * Good organisational and time management skills. * Adaptable and able to work cross\-functionally. * Competent use of Microsoft Office. * Maintains confidentiality and has a professional manner. **Main Responsibilities** * Provide clinical Support to Regulatory specialist in preparation of clinical trial submissions to the GMS * Maintain and expand nutrition knowledge of the Abbott Nutrition team through training and CPD development * Provide a scientific Medical Nutrition service for marketed products, ensuring it is promoted in a professional manner and is delivered in line with contractual requirements. To work collaboratively with the marketing team and other support functions to support product positioning and claims development. * Develop e\-learning modules and nutrition related materials for supporting training needs of HCP’s * Deliver education and training to internal and external customers. * Maintain corporate image by representing Abbott Nutrition at scientific meetings and exhibitions. * Provide support to Commercial Lead for onboarding Graduate Dietitian * Along with Commercial Lead create a plan for dietetic student training (approx. 4 students per year) **What We Offer** Attractive compensation package that includes **competitive pay and structure Bonus**, as well as benefits such as * Private Health Insurance for you and your family * Occupational Pension Scheme with an excellence contribution rate * Bonus Target * Life assurance * Career Development * Growing business plus access to many more benefits Connect with us at www.abbott.com, on LinkedIn at www.linkedin.com/company/abbott\-/, on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Abbott and on Twitter @AbbottNews and @AbbottGlobal.

title: Early Years Educator Graduate
description: **Graduate Early Years Educator – Lead Role in Dublin 12** **Cover Staff Dublin 5** **Best Start Childcare: Where You Matter.** **Location:** Dublin 12 \& Dublin 5 **Type:** Full\-time, Permanent, Contract **Start Date:** ASAP – April 27th 2026 **Be Heard. Be Valued. Be Happy.** At Best Start Childcare, we believe the best care for children starts with the best care for our team. We provide a nurturing and inclusive space where every educator feels empowered and appreciated. **THE OPPORTUNITY** We are seeking a qualified and passionate **Graduate Early Years Educator** to take a lead role. This is your chance to guide a high\-quality play\-based curriculum and grow your leadership skills in a respectful, professional environment. **WHAT WE OFFER** ✅**TIME FOR YOU:** We offer a dedicated **Wellness Day** and performance\-based leave because your rest is important. ✅**PEACE OF MIND:** Access to an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and 5 days of sick pay. ✅ **EXPERT MENTORING:** Professional growth and on\-site mentoring from the **Better Start Quality Support Team**. ✅**SUPPORTIVE CULTURE:** A warm, team\-oriented workplace with regular company events and a focus on collaboration. ✅**EXCLUSIVE BENEFITS:** Fantastic discounts on holidays and shopping through our **Bright HR platform**. **REQUIREMENTS** * **Level 7 (or higher)** in Early Childhood Education. * **2–3 years’ experience** in an early years setting. * **Strong knowledge** of Aistear/Siolta and a team\-oriented mindset. **HOW TO APPLY** **Please send your CV to: careers@beststartchildcare.ie** Job Types: Full\-time, Graduate Benefits: * Additional leave * Company events * Employee assistance program Experience: * Childcare: 1 year (required) Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Fixed term, Graduate Contract length: 12 months Pay: From €17\.50 per hour Benefits: * Additional leave * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Sick pay Education: * Bachelor's (required) Experience: * childcare Ireland : 2 years (required) Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Ecommerce & Omnichannel Marketing Coordinator
description: **Contract Type:** Full time, permanent **Location:** M7 Business Park, Naas, Co Kildare **Hours:** 37\.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Flexibility in working hours may be required to meet the needs of the organisation. **Department:** Business Development **Reports to:** Omnichannel Marketing \& E\-commerce Manager **Closing date**: 9th April 2026 ### **Role Overview** The E\-commerce \& Omnichannel Marketing Coordinator will support the delivery and growth of Vision Ireland's online retail operations across Shopify and third\-party marketplaces. This role is suited to a recent graduate or early\-career professional looking to build practical experience in e\-commerce, digital marketing, and online trading. The role is primarily focused on online trading and product performance, with additional responsibility for supporting digital marketing execution. The successful candidate will contribute to product listings, website merchandising, campaign support, and performance tracking, while developing a strong understanding of what drives online sales and customer engagement. ### **Key Responsibilities** **E\-commerce \& Trading (70%)** * Create, optimise, and publish product listings across Shopify and resale platforms (eBay, Depop, Vinted, Vestiaire) * Ensure all listings are accurate, complete, and aligned with best practice (titles, descriptions, images, pricing, and attributes) * Maintain consistency of product data across all platforms * Support inventory updates, stock flow, and bulk uploads * Review and improve existing listings to enhance visibility and performance * Assist with website merchandising, including collections, categories, and featured products * Monitor product performance and highlight slow\-moving or high\-performing items * Support pricing updates, promotions, and relisting of older inventory **Digital Marketing Support (30%)** * Assist in the execution of email campaigns and relevant marketing content * Support product selection and coordination for campaigns * Ensure alignment between marketing activity and website content * Implement basic SEO tasks such as meta descriptions and image alt text * Support campaign setup, QA, and scheduling **Marketplace \& Platform Support** * Apply platform\-specific best practices to improve listing visibility and performance * Support the use of marketplace tools such as promotions, offers, and relisting * Monitor basic performance KPIs including views, clicks, and sales * Maintain an active and relevant product catalogue across all channels **Customer Experience \& Website Support** * Review product pages and identify issues affecting usability or clarity * Support improvements to site navigation and product presentation * Assist with customer queries where required * Ensure content is clear, accurate, and accessible **Reporting** * Support weekly reporting on sales and product performance * Track key metrics such as sales, conversion rate, and stock movement * Highlight trends and opportunities for improvement Maintain simple trackers using Excel and platform analytics tools * *This is not an exhaustive list of duties. The successful candidate must be open to flexibility in their duties in line with the needs of the organisation.* ### **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Degree or Professional qualifications in Business, Marketing, Digital, Retail, or a related field * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Interest in e\-commerce, retail, or fashion * Good working knowledge of Excel or similar tools * Strong communication skills and willingness to learn **Desirable** * Experience with Shopify or similar e\-commerce platforms * Familiarity with resale marketplaces (eBay, Depop, Vinted, Vestiaire) * Basic understanding of digital marketing or SEO * Previous retail or customer\-facing experience **What This Role Offers** * Hands\-on experience across multiple e\-commerce platforms * Exposure to digital trading, merchandising, and marketing * Opportunity to develop commercial awareness and analytical skills * A clear pathway into a more senior e\-commerce or digital marketing role **Success Measures** Success in this role will be measured by: * Accuracy and quality of product listings * Volume and efficiency of product uploads * Improvements in product visibility and engagement * Contribution to overall online sales performance Ability to take ownership of tasks and work independently * ### **Further Information for Candidates:** Vision Ireland reserve the right to close this competition early should an adequate number of applications be received. All applicants must submit a current CV. Candidates must be eligible to work full\-time in Ireland. Further information about Vision Ireland can be found on our website www.vi.ie Informal enquiries to recruitment@vi.ie Vision Ireland is an equal opportunities employer. **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** Vision Ireland is committed to creating an inclusive environment where diversity is celebrated, and everyone is afforded equality of opportunity. It is our policy to recognise people as a key resource required for successful attainment of the organisation's mission. In support of this, it is important to remember that differences between people, whether devised from their different backgrounds and personalities, cultures and/or their different abilities, can be a source of strength to the organisation. We welcome, encourage, and embrace people of all backgrounds, to include those with disabilities. Accessibility plays a huge part of ensuring that all our employees and service users can access our systems and services with ease and respect, in a supportive environment, \- enabling all to effectively engage our services and achieve our goals whether they are an applicant for employment or an employee requiring special facilitation.

title: Emergency Medicine Fellowship Ambulatory Emergency Care - July 2026
description: Overview: **BEAUMONT HOSPITAL****(Incorporating St Joseph’s Hospital, Raheny)** Beaumont Hospital, incorporating St. Joseph’s Hospital Raheny, is a large academic teaching hospital 5km north of Dublin City centre. Beaumont Hospital provides emergency and acute care services across 54 medical specialties to a local community of some 290,000 people. In addition, Beaumont Hospital is a designated cancer centre and the regional treatment centre for ear, nose and throat, and gastroenterology, and the national referral centre for neurosurgery and neurology, renal transplantation, cochlear implantation and mechanical thrombectomy. Beaumont Hospital is the lead Level 4 hospital within the Royal College of Surgeons Hospital Group, which includes Connolly Hospital, Rotunda Hospital, Cavan/Monaghan Hospitals, Louth/Meath Hospitals, and the Royal College of Surgeons. Beaumont Hospital employs approximately 4,000 staff and has a bed complement of 820 beds. Beaumont Hospital is the principal teaching hospital for the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. Beaumont Hospital also enjoys close links with Dublin City University, especially in the area of nurse training, and with other academic institutions in respect of training and research. St. Joseph's Hospital Raheny, is an acute hospital that provides both medical and surgical in\-patient care, day care, outpatient, outpatient physiotherapy and radiology services. A dedicated Care of the Elderly Rehabilitation Unit and a 100 bedded Raheny Community Nursing Unit have also been developed on the St. Joseph’s campus. **Emergency Medicine Directorate Fellowship \-** **Ambulatory Emergency Care****.** **Duration of the Fellowship:** 12 months \- July 2026 to July 2027 **Location/Clinical Site of the Fellowship**Beaumont Hospital, incorporating Emergency Medicine, Neurosurgery and Neurocritical Care. **Fellowship Aims and Overview:** The overall aim of this fellowship is to provide advanced, structured training in the area of Ambulatory Emergency Care. The Fellow will develop a consultant\-level skillset that enhances the clinical experience and outcomes for patients availing of the current ambulatory emergency care area within the Emergency Department (ED). This Fellowship will compromise of a 12 month attachment to the ED and incorporate the following core elements: * Ambulatory and Urgent Emergency Care – the Fellow will work within the ambulatory emergency care area of the ED providing high level expert care to a large patient volume, ensuring streaming from both the triage and post triage spaces, targeting the delivery of safe, efficient and effective care. * Development of Clinical Care Pathways including ‘Alternative Pathways’– the Fellow will expect to gain experience in and assist in the development of Clinical Care Pathways and Alternative Pathways to ED attendance, and work collaboratively with the community and the Acute Admissions Unit, as well as other relevant stakeholders at Beaumont Hospital, in the development of admission avoidance strategies. * Performance Metric Analysis and Monitoring – the Fellow will contribute to and enhance the capture of relevant performance data for the ambulatory emergency care area, contribute to monthly clinical governance meetings and work closely with the wider EM multidiscliniary group to ensure care standards are continuously met and improved . The Fellow will participate in consultant\-led teaching, lead on relevant teaching on pathways development, attend and contribute to relevant governance meetings, as well as national and international meetings. Supervision will be provided by Consultant’s in Emergency Medicine as well as the wider EM Directorate Team **Candidate Profile and Anticipated Working Week:** The successful applicant will hold (or intend to hold prior to commencement of the Fellowship) a CCST in Emergency Medicine. The successful Fellow will work 30 hours per week on the clinical floor with 9 additional hours dedicated to clinical pathway development, research, clinical audit, quality improvement and teaching. Post Specifications: * Each candidate **must** hold a valid **Irish Medical Council Registration** * MRCEM Primary examination * English Language Competency: All doctors taking up employment who were not registered with the Irish Medical Council in any of the divisions of the Register of Medical Practitioners prior to **1st January 2015**, or who did not complete the entirety of their under\-graduate medical training in the Republic of Ireland, are required to provide evidence of one of the following: * Completion of your Medical degree in English from any of the following countries: Australia, Canada, New Zealand, The United States of America or The United Kingdom. A copy of your Degree Certificate is the required evidence **OR*** English Language Tests Certificate: International English Language Test System Certificate (IELTS – minimum overall band score of 7\.0\) **or** Occupational English Test Certificate (OET – minimum overall grade score of B). IELTS/OET test results must be current and dated within two years of the date submitted to the employer. The Emergency Department (ED) in Beaumont Hospital provides care to 60,000 patients each year. The adult only ED is based in an Academic Teaching Hospital with an available bed base of in excess of 800 beds. The Consultant team have been involved in core and higher specialist training in Emergency Medicine for over two decades. There is protected Consultant delivered teaching each week. The ED has an active research and audit programme and the Consultant team have supported Doctors in the performance of research from the concept phase to international presentation and publication in the peer reviewed literature. The Hospital has a Neuroscience centre, Neurosurgical Unit, Oncology and Radiation Oncology Centre and national ENT centre with a very active Stroke Unit and most specialty services are available on site. We take pride in providing high quality care to our patients and clinical support for our Doctors in training. **Closing date for receipt of applications is 2pm on Friday 3rd April 2026\.** Applicants should note that it will not be possible to inform all applicants individually of the outcome of their applications. **Please note that some of the above posts may be filled by recognised training schemes prior to any hospital interviews.****Beaumont Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and supports a no smoking policy**

The Fellow will develop a consultant\-level skillset that improves patient outcomes and contribute to the enhancement of neurotrauma and neurocritical care provision in Ireland. This Fellowship will compromise of two 6 months rotations: * Emergency Medicine – 6 months: this component focuses on early and effective neuro\-resuscitation, trauma airway management, ventilation strategies for severe TBI and the optimisation of physiological parameters known to influence secondary brain injury, as well as the standardisation and development of relevant neurotrauma and neurocritical care pathways between the ED, the wider hospital and relevant national stakeholders. * Neurocritical Care and Neurosurgery – 6 months: this attachment will facilitate high level training in the management of severe TBI and neurocritically unwell patients in the Richmond and General ICUs, as well as exposure to both operative and non\-operative management strategies for this patient cohort. The Fellow will gain experience in the interpretation of neuroimaging by attending MDMs and participate in relevant neurosurgical interventions, ward rounds and multidisciplinary meeting Th e Fellow will participate in consultant\-led teaching, lead on interdisciplinary simulation training in neurotrauma and neuro\-resuscitation, attend and contribute to relevant governance meetings, as well as national and international meetings. Supervision will be provided by Consultant’s in Emergency Medicine supported by a Consultant Neurosurgeon and a Consultant Neuroanaesthesiologist / Intensivist. **Candidate Profile and Anticipated Working Week:** The successful applicant will hold (or intend to hold prior to commencement of the Fellowship) a CCST in Emergency Medicine. The successful Fellow will work 30 hours per week on the clinical floor with 9 additional hours dedicated to clinical pathway development, research, clinical audit, quality improvement and interdisciplinary teaching. A copy of your Degree Certificate is the required evidence **OR** * English Language Tests Certificate: International English Language Test System Certificate (IELTS – minimum overall band score of 7\.0\) or Occupational English Test Certificate (OET – minimum overall grade score of B). IELTS/OET test results must be current and dated within two years of the date submitted to the employer. The Emergency Department (ED) in Beaumont Hospital provides care to 60,000 patients each year. The adult only ED is based in an Academic Teaching Hospital with an available bed base of in excess of 800 beds. The Consultant team have been involved in core and higher specialist training in Emergency Medicine for over two decades. There is protected Consultant delivered teaching each week. The ED has an active research and audit programme and the Consultant team have supported Doctors in the performance of research from the concept phase to international presentation and publication in the peer reviewed literature. The Hospital has a Neuroscience centre, Neurosurgical Unit, Oncology and Radiation Oncology Centre and national ENT centre with a very active Stroke Unit and most specialty services are available on site. We take pride in providing high quality care to our patients and clinical support for our Doctors in training. **Closing date for receipt of applications is 2pm on Friday 3rd April 2026\.** Applicants should note that it will not be possible to inform all applicants individually of the outcome of their applications. **Please note that some of the above posts may be filled by recognised training schemes prior to any hospital interviews.** **Beaumont Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and supp orts a no smoking policy**

title: Environmental Scientist
description: **Environmental Scientist** We’re looking for an experienced and motivated **Environmental Scientist** to join our team. We have a number of roles available and have offices around the country. This role focuses on the successful delivery of **contaminated land remediation projects**, with a strong emphasis the remediation of land, water and buildings following oil contamination. If you have a passion for environmental science and thrive in a dynamic environment, this could be the perfect opportunity for you. **About the Role:** You’ll play a key role in delivering site investigation and remediation projects, both in the field and from the office. The position involves regular travel, and a company vehicle is provided. We're looking for someone who’s proactive, curious, and always eager to learn — qualities we actively support through a comprehensive **personal development programme**. **Key Responsibilities:** * Deliver technical proposals for contaminated land investigations and remediation, particularly oil spill projects. * Manage the scoping, preparation, and execution of site assessments and remediation plans. * Supervise field service teams in sampling techniques, monitoring, and response actions. * Prepare detailed technical reports, remediation option appraisals and programmes. * Conduct soil waste classification and data analysis to support environmental decision\-making. * Liaise with clients, regulatory bodies, and stakeholders as project needs require. * Direct and deliver field activities including soil and groundwater testing, air quality monitoring, and remediation actions. * Support and mentor junior team members, providing oversight and guidance. * Collaborate closely with the Project Management team to ensure successful project outcomes. **What We’re Looking For:** * A minimum of 3 years’ experience working in contaminated land, with solid knowledge of oil contamination and remediation methods. * Strong scientific background and analytical skills. * Comfortable working in both office and field environments. * Valid driver’s license (required). * A team player with excellent communication and leadership skills. **What We Offer:** * Flexible / remote working options * Competitive salary (commensurate with experience) * Company vehicle and fuel card * Annual performance bonus * Pension scheme * Income protection \& death\-in\-service insurance * Company phone and laptop * Gym access and bike\-to\-work scheme * Ongoing learning and development support Join us and be part of a team that values your growth and contributions while working on impactful environmental projects. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company car * Company events * Company pension * Food allowance * On\-site gym * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Work from home Experience: * environmental consultancy: 2 years (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Kilcoole, County Wicklow

title: Environmental Technician
description: Overview: Element has an opportunity for an **Environmental Technician** whose prime responsibility is to assist the regional environmental field services department in environmental testing and reporting at project sites. Responsibilities: * Assist and learn all aspects of work the environmental department carry out including stack emissions monitoring, ambient air quality monitoring, soil and groundwater monitoring and noise monitoring * A willingness to embrace new skills in the above mentioned areas and apply them in a practical context is considered prerequisite * Assist with planning and execution of projects * Assist with report preparation * Assistance with maintenance and calibration of environmental department equipment * Assistance with maintenance of Health and Safety and Quality standards within a UKAS accredited 17025 quality management systems. Skills / Qualifications: * Science Graduate or higher qualification * 2 years relevant experience * Physical fitness and ability to withstand the challenging working conditions presented by the climbing of stacks and handling heavy industrial * Strong and determined quality focus with indefatigable determination to get the job done under the most challenging conditions * Self\-motivated team playing with excellent organizational and communication skills * Willingness to embrace new skills and apply them in a practical context is considered prerequisite Company Overview: Element is one of the fastest growing testing, inspection and certification businesses in the world. Globally we have more than 9,000 brilliant minds operating from 270 sites across 30 countries. Together we share an ambitious purpose to ‘Make tomorrow safer than today’. When failure in use is not an option, we help customers make certain that their products, materials, processes and services are safe, compliant and fit for purpose. From early R\&D, through complex regulatory approvals and into production, our global laboratory network of scientists, engineers, and technologists support customers to achieve assurance over product quality, sustainable outcomes, and market access. While we are proud of our global reach, working at Element feels like being part of a smaller company. We empower you to take charge of your career, and reward excellence and integrity with growth and development. Industries across the world depend on our care, attention to detail and the absolute accuracy of our work. The role we have to play in creating a safer world is much bigger than our organization. Diversity Statement: At Element, we always take pride in putting our people first. We are an equal opportunity employer that recognizes diversity and inclusion as fundamental to our Vision of becoming “the world’s most trusted testing partner”. All suitably qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment on the basis of objective work related criteria and without regard for the following: age, disability, ethnic origin, gender, marital status, race, religion, responsibility of dependents, sexual orientation, or gender identity or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws.

title: Event Driven Research Analyst | Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. As an analyst on Susquehanna’s Research team, you will partner with our senior analysts and traders to identify investment opportunities in the equity or options market. Research analysts provide real\-time opinions on breaking news and in\-depth analysis of future events. You will be responsible for finding opportunities that other market participants may not be capitalising on, either by discovering new information or considering existing knowledge from a different perspective. This role provides the unique opportunity to combine the quantitative aspects of finance with creative problem\-solving in order to develop trade ideas in a fast\-paced, proprietary trading environment. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Evaluating and tracking special situations such as mergers, acquisitions, and other corporate actions * Identifying and analysing upcoming events * Providing real\-time opinions on breaking news * Collaborating with traders on investment ideas * Traveling to company events and conferences as required * Performing stock valuation analysis **What We're Looking For** * Minimum of a bachelor's degree * Minimum 1 year Experience * CFA or part\-qualified CFA Candidate preferred * Strong written, analytical, and financial research skills * Ability to prioritize and complete multiple tasks in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment * Prior exposure to fundamental company research and event driven analysis an advantage * Familiarity with a trading environment and derivative products preferred * A self\-starter, quick learner, effective communicator, and problem solver * Willingness to work additional and flexible hours when necessary If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com . Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Global Health Advisor
description: **The Role** **About Concern:** Established in 1968, Concern Worldwide is an international, humanitarian, non\-governmental organisation dedicated to the elimination of extreme poverty and the reduction of human suffering, primarily in fragile countries. Our Vision is a world where all people live in peace and prosperity and are treated equally, with dignity and respect. Informed by the priorities of affected communities, we go where the needs are greatest, and work to save lives, protect and develop livelihoods, support recovery and build long\-term resilience To advance our mission and ensure high quality‑quality, impactful work, we are guided by our core values and a culture committed to inclusion, compassion, and humanity. Inspired by our founders and our long history of effective programming, these values reflect both who we are and how we address extreme poverty in the world’s most fragile countries. We are dedicated to fostering a workplace where everyone feels valued and supported to succeed and contribute to our mission. The Strategy Advocacy and Learning Department (SAL) supports the whole organisation in developing and setting strategy and supporting implementation of the same, assuring programme impact, supporting advocacy and influencing and facilitating and driving organisational learning. The Technical Assistance (TA) Unit provides direct assistance to Country Programmes and comprises over 30 advisors who support ongoing learning, programme quality and capacity development. **Role Purpose:** To provide high‑quality technical leadership in line with Concern’s Health \& Nutrition Strategy 2026–2030, supporting country programmes to strengthen primary health care systems, deliver integrated health and nutrition programming, improve quality of care, and enhance preparedness and response to health and nutrition emergencies. The role acts as a global resource person for multiple country programmes. **Skills and Experience** **Responsibilities:** **Delivering the Technical Strategy** * Support delivery of annual workplans aligned with the Health \& Nutrition Strategy 2026–2030, including PHC strengthening, quality improvement, behaviour change and gender‑transformative approaches, and lead on specific activities/ initiatives as agreed with the Senior Health Adviser. * Ensure health programming reflects Concern’s organisational priorities for health and nutrition and support teams to identify and resources the technical skills needed to deliver them. **Direct Technical Support to Country Programmes** * Serve as focal point for a portfolio of countries, providing timely and responsive remote and in‑country technical support across programme design, implementation and review. * Support integration of health, nutrition and WASH and promote equitable access, locally led programming and improved quality of PHC services (including SRH), and strengthened CHW/community systems. **Capacity Building \& Support** * Lead technical support and capacity building for focal countries and more broadly in agreed technical areas, including coaching, training, and joint supervision on PHC quality improvement, Concern Surge Approach integration and data use. * Promote locally led approaches and build strong collaboration with MoH and local partners. **Learning, Guidance \& Knowledge Management** * Document and share learning across countries, producing guidance, tools and learning products relevant to PHC, integrated health–nutrition and disease surveillance preparedness and response. * Contribute to evaluations, reviews and meta‑evaluations related to the strategy. * Support the continuous development of Concern’s work around climate and health linked to wash. **Proposal Development \& Technical Review** * Provide technical inputs to health components of proposals, concept notes and donor reports. * Ensure design and resources reflect strategy priorities and minimum criteria for higher‑risk interventions. * Contribute to identification of strategic health funding opportunities. **Representation \& External Engagement** * Represent Concern in technical working groups, networks and coordination fora relevant to PHC (especially SRH), nutrition integration and emergency preparedness. * Build strategic relationships with technical agencies, research institutions and donors. **Organisational Responsibilities** * Ensure Concern’s values, safeguarding, accountability, equality and inclusion are embedded in all technical support. * Support emergency responses as required and contribute to cross‑sectoral analyses and assessments. **Role Holder Requirements:** **Essential:** * Undergraduate degree/diploma in nursing, medicine or midwifery or post‑graduate qualification in public health. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience in health programming and technical advisory roles in or supporting fragile or conflict‑affected contexts across a range of countries. * Strong understanding of PHC systems (including SRH), health and nutrition integration, quality improvement and health emergency preparedness. **Other** We encourage all eligible candidates, irrespective of gender, ethnicity or origin, disability, political beliefs, religious beliefs, sexual orientation, or socio\-economic status to apply to become a part of the organisation. Concern is against all forms of discrimination and unequal power relations and is committed to promoting equality. If this role sounds right for you, please apply with your CV and cover letter. We will respond to every applicant. Please be aware we may offer positions before the closing date. If you have any concerns about our recruitment process and need assistance \- for example if you have a disability e.g. a hearing impairment \- please let us know and we will do our best to respond to your needs. **To apply:** CVs should be submitted through our website at https://jobs.concern.net by the closing date. **Please note that Concern Worldwide does not provide Visa sponsorship.** Due to the urgency of this position, applications will be shortlisted on a regular basis and we may offer posts before the closing date. If you have any concerns about our recruitment process and need particular assistance \- for example if you have a visual impairment or are neurodivergent \- please let us know and we will do our best to accommodate you. **Important Information:** Concern has an organisational **Code of Conduct** with three Associated Policies: The **Programme Participant Protection Policy, the Child Safeguarding Policy, and the Anti\-Trafficking in Persons Policy** accessible here. These have been developed to ensure the maximum protection of programme participants from exploitation, and to clarify the responsibilities of Concern staff, consultants, visitors to the programme and partner organisations, and the standards of behaviour expected of them. In this context, staff have a responsibility to the organisation to strive for, and maintain, the highest standards in their work, in accordance with Concern’s core values and mission. Any candidate offered a job with Concern Worldwide will be expected to sign the Concern Staff Code of Conduct and Associated Policies as an appendix to their contract of employment. By signing the Concern Code of Conduct, candidates acknowledge that they have understood the content of both the Concern Code of Conduct and the Associated Policies and agree to conduct themselves in accordance with the provisions of these policies. Additionally, Concern is committed to the safeguarding and protection of adults and children in our work. We will do everything possible to ensure that only those who are suitable to work or volunteer with adults and children are recruited by us for such roles. Consequently, working or volunteering with Concern may be subject to a range of vetting checks, including criminal background checking. During this job application, you will provide Concern with your **personal data**. Concern takes its responsibilities towards this personal data very seriously and is committed to complying with all relevant data protection legislation. Job Reference: YRPO00375

Abbott also has commercial, support operations and shared services in Dublin and Galway. We have been making a difference in the lives of Irish people since 1946\. **Abbott Ireland Commercial Liffey Valley Dublin** Opened in 1946, this operation, based in Liffey Valley Dublin was the first Abbott business unit to be established in Ireland. This business is responsible for the sales, marketing and distribution of a wide range of diabetes, diagnostic, and nutritional products in Ireland. This role will be office based in The Liffey Valley office. **Position : Abbott Nutrition Dietetic Graduate (14 Months) Abbott Ireland** The Graduate Dietitian, referred to as a **Nutrition Specialist** until CORU registration is complete, is a key member of the Abbott Nutrition Ireland team. This role supports the delivery of high‑quality, evidence‑based nutrition services, ensures the accuracy and compliance of nutrition communication, and contributes to regulatory, clinical, and commercial activities across the organisation. The position offers an excellent opportunity for a newly qualified dietitian to apply clinical and scientific knowledge in a commercial healthcare environment while developing professional expertise in medical nutrition. **Key Responsibilities** * Review and approve content for Abbott Nutrition marketing materials to ensure accuracy, scientific integrity, and compliance with relevant standards and guidelines. * Conduct literature searches to support internal team needs or specific requests from healthcare professionals. * Translate complex scientific evidence into clear, accurate, and clinically relevant messaging. * Operate a dietetic helpline providing high‑quality nutrition advice to healthcare professionals across Ireland. * Respond to enquiries from healthcare providers with evidence‑based, professional guidance. * Work closely with line manager to support regulatory submissions and reimbursement applications for Abbott Nutrition products available in Ireland. * Assist in maintaining documentation and compliance aligned with regulatory, CORU, and company standards. **Personal Attributes** * The ideal candidate will demonstrate : * Strong academic performance combined with extracurricular or voluntary achievements. * Ability to quickly learn and accurately interpret technical and scientific information. * Excellent communication skills with the ability to build rapport with internal stakeholders and external healthcare professionals. * High proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). * Strong organisation, time management, and project management skills. * Ability to work effectively under pressure and meet deadlines. * A collaborative, team‑oriented approach. * Professionalism, discretion, and the ability to maintain confidentiality at all times. **Required Professional Qualifications** * Level 8 BSc (Hons) in Human Nutrition \& Dietetics (or equivalent qualification). * Evidence of CORU application (required at job‑offer stage). **Additional Information** * The successful candidate will hold the title Nutrition Specialist until a CORU registration number is issued. * The role provides significant exposure to clinical nutrition, medical devices, product regulation, and commercial healthcare environments—ideal for a graduate seeking broad experience while developing professional dietetic practice. **What We Offer** * Attractive compensation package that includes competitive pay and structure Bonus, as well as benefits such as * Private Health Insurance for you and your family * Occupational Pension Scheme with an excellence contribution rate * Bonus Target * Life assurance * Career Development * Growing business plus access to many more benefits
