title: Senior EHS Advisor
description: **Job Summary** The Senior EHS Advisor plays a crucial role in maintaining and improving health, safety, and environmental standards across multiple construction projects. This position involves working closely with site teams to ensure compliance with regulations, conducting risk assessments, and fostering a culture of safety. The Senior EHS Advisor provides expert guidance on risk reduction strategies, ensures all personnel receive adequate training, and supports the EHS Manager in driving continuous improvement initiatives. Additionally, they act as a key liaison with regulatory bodies and contribute to the implementation of sustainability and environmental initiatives. **Key Activities** * Conduct regular site audits and inspections to ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations, as well as company procedures. * Develop, review, and update risk assessments and method statements to identify and mitigate potential hazards. * Deliver training sessions and toolbox talks to site personnel and subcontractors, promoting best practices in workplace safety. * Investigate incidents, accidents, and near\-misses, preparing detailed reports with recommendations for corrective actions. * Ensure all necessary safety signage, PPE, and emergency procedures are in place and adhered to on\-site. * Collaborate with project management teams to implement and monitor site\-specific Construction Health and Safety Plans. * Enforce compliance with company policies, industry standards, and HSE legislation across all projects. * Support environmental initiatives, including waste management, pollution control, and energy efficiency measures. * Maintain and manage HSE documentation, such as statutory inspections, permits, incident logs, and audit reports, ensuring accuracy and accessibility. * Act as the primary point of contact for regulatory bodies during site visits and inspections. * Provide proactive advice on safety performance improvement and risk mitigation strategies. * Act as a manager for specific direct reports, including Graduate EHS Advisors and Student EHS placements. * Manage workload across multiple sites. * Provide training and support for HammerTech for both new and existing staff. * Assist with incident investigations when required. * Support ISO site audit preparation for each assessment cycle. * Attend high\-level meetings with senior management, including client\-facing discussions and tender clarifications. * Continue involvement with IGBC and support in Sustainability/ESG tasks, including tenders and pre\-qualifications. * Undertake additional instructor training, such as Working at Heights and Fire Awareness. **Competencies** * Comprehensive knowledge of EHS regulations, risk assessment, and hazard identification * Proficiency in incident investigation, root cause analysis, and safety performance data analysis * Expertise in management systems (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 45001\) * Effective collaboration with project teams, subcontractors, and staff training on EHS practices * Adaptability to evolving regulations, project requirements, and proactive improvement initiatives * Strong organisational skills, communication skills, attention to detail, and commitment to safety excellence **Education \& Experience** * Level 8 or above in environmental science, Occupational Health and Safety, or a related field (or equivalent qualifications) * Prior experience in EHS roles, preferably within the construction industry (internships or entry\-level roles acceptable). * NEBOSH National General Certificate or equivalent qualification is an advantage. * Familiarity with HSE management systems and basic compliance audits is desirable. * Practical knowledge of construction safety regulations and environmental legislation. * Interest in developing skills in incident investigation, safety training, and environmental management. * Commitment to continuous professional development in EHS\-related topics. **Benefits** * 21 days annual leave, with additional days accrued for long service * Company pension scheme * Company vehicle provided for site travel * Laptop and mobile phone supplied * Company sick pay scheme * Statutory and enhanced maternity and paternity pay * Wedding leave * Cycle to Work Scheme * Comprehensive training and development opportunities, including CPD and instructor training * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for confidential support * Regular company social events and wellbeing initiatives

title: Senior Engineer
description: Title: Senior Engineer Requisition ID: 10543 **Amey Consulting – Senior Engineer** We have a fantastic opportunity for a permanent **Senior Engineer** to join our Consulting Highways team in **Dublin** . The standard hours of work are 40 Hours per week, but we offer a range of flexible working practices, including hybrid, part\-time and flexible working patterns. With our network of offices across the UK and now Ireland, we are open to discussing working options that match your needs, with the flexibility to change how you work as your life evolves. **What You Will Be Doing** Our Senior Engineers play an important part within Highways team. As a Senior Civil Engineer, you will lead junior engineers in designing and delivering a variety of highway and public transport projects, including cycleways, greenways, junction improvements, highway drainage/SUDs, bus priority measures, pavement schemes, maintenance programs, and major roads or bridges. As part of our Dublin team, you’ll work on local projects and support initiatives in Ireland and the UK remotely. **Your Responsibilities Will Include** * Lead on the delivery of scheme design, as well as providing support to the Design Team Leader within the design team on various design duties including: * Surveys/Inspections * Feasibility and other technical reports * Planning, programming and cost estimation * Design production * Production of design schedules * Reviewing and amending design drawings * Production of Specifications * Supporting development of Graduate Engineers and Technicians * Work to programme to deliver specified designs on time * Drive effective health and safety practices within your team * Liaise with the Client and our Community partners to ensure solutions that are developed are suitable for buildability and maintainability * Prepare technical reports (e.g. Feasibility/Options Reports, Assessment Reports and other technical reports) **We want to hear from you if you have** * Developed experience and expertise within Highways/Civil engineering field, probably gained in an Engineer role or equivalent * Comprehensive knowledge of design processes and procedures relevant to the civil engineering discipline * An ability to produce or oversee the production of technically sound Highways engineering solutions, complying with all relevant technical standards and client requirements * Experience in undertaking project related tasks associated with health, safety and welfare, e.g. Construction (Design and Management) Regulations * Successful completion of the initial professional development leading to either Incorporated or Chartered status or an equivalent professional licence and to the level defined by their education * A demonstrable commitment to their plan of actions and key dates leading to successful completion of either the professional review or end point assessment, resulting in the award of Incorporated or Chartered status or an equivalent professional licence * Bachelors or Masters degree in an appropriate subject or demonstrable equivalent further learning * IEng or CEng status is desirable, gained through the professional institution to which the individual is aligned We welcome applications from a diverse range of candidates. If you have most but not all of these skills and experiences, we encourage you to apply, and we will provide you with development or training to support you. **What We Can Offer** Our benefits are designed to help you thrive at work and in your home life. You will have the flexibility to choose benefits and development opportunities that are best for you. **Some Of Our Benefits Include** * Exceptional personal development, training and progression plans, designed to allow you to shine in your career, all in line with your personal ambition * Competitive salary and compensation * Generous pension scheme, with extra contributions from Amey * 24 days annual leave, plus bank holidays * Flexible benefits scheme, including the option to buy additional annual leave and insurance benefits, Cycle2Work scheme and access to discounted gym membership * Our Save with Amey programme gives you access to thousands of discounts from leading retailers to help you save on shopping, days out, or nights in * Family friendly policies for new parents or if you provide care for a dependant * Membership of our Affinity Networks who connect, support and inspire diverse communities within Amey * Two Social Impact Days each year, for volunteering and fundraising opportunities **About Amey** **Who we are** Amey is a global, integrated infrastructure consultancy and operations partner. We design, deliver, protect, and maintain critical assets and systems. **What We Do** We advise, design, finance, deliver, and enhance civic, transport, and defence systems. By combining strategic intelligence, engineering, infrastructure finance, and operational expertise, we create secure, resilient, compliant, and high\-value outcomes across the lifecycles of new and legacy assets. To find out more visit our website amey.co.uk/careers **Application Guidance** **Diversity and Inclusion** We constantly evolve how we work to reflect the different needs and backgrounds of our employees. We seek out the skills, joy and energy that often\-marginalised groups can bring to our teams and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, regardless of their race, gender, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or age. We are committed to offering applicants with a disability an interview if they meet the minimum requirements for the role. Please contact our recruitment team at peopleservices@amey.co.uk to discuss any access needs, reasonable adjustments or additional support that you may require at any point during the recruitment process. **Apply today** We may close this vacancy early if we receive sufficient applications. Please apply as soon as possible. We look forward to hearing from you. **Inspiring Impact Together**

title: Senior Engineer, Site Reliability
description: LearnUpon is looking for a Senior Engineer, Site Reliability to join our team in Dublin. This is a flex role, working 1 day per week from LearnUpon's Dublin office. LearnUpon LMS helps organizations train their employees, partners, and customers. Businesses can manage, track, and achieve their unique learning goals — all through a single, powerful solution. With offices in Dublin (our HQ), Belgrade, Philadelphia, Salt Lake City and Sydney, we are a global team with lots of diverse cultures, backgrounds, and experiences that puts our customers' experience at the heart of everything we do. Our culture fosters an open, collaborative and supportive environment where our accomplishments are celebrated and encouraged. We're always striving for the best solution (not the easy one). We're proud of our success and we're humble and hungry to achieve more. #### **About the team:** You will be part of the SRE Team, which sits within LearnUpon's Engineering group. We are a small team focused on developing and supporting our cloud infrastructure and app services, to ensure platform scalability and site uptime. Our flagship product is coded predominantly in Ruby on Rails, with data managed through a common mix of current SaaS back\-end technologies including AWS backed services. We also use local containerised development environments. However, we are not bound to our tech stack. We prefer choosing the right technology for the right problem so you'll have plenty of space to grow your skills. We are key consultants for the entire company on matters of infrastructure feasibility. #### **What will I be doing?** As a Senior Site Reliability Engineer you will be responsible for the day\-to\-day operation and management of the LearnUpon platform infrastructure. You will be designing, implementing and maintaining a highly available and scalable infrastructure with the primary focus on planning ahead for our future as we look to transition towards a containerized environment. While our tool stack is predominantly AWS, Terraform and Ansible, we welcome anyone with experience with similar technologies to be part of our journey. We prefer choosing the right technology for the right problem so you'll have plenty of space to grow your skills. * Identifying opportunities to improve and scale our infrastructure for performance, observability, maintainability, and cost, by creating innovative solutions with a strong emphasis on infrastructure as code. * Ensure System Reliability and Efficiency: Continuously monitor and improve the reliability, scalability, and performance of our SaaS platform, ensuring high availability and optimal functioning of services in a cloud\-based environment. * Incident Management and Response: Lead and participate in incident response and post\-mortem analysis to effectively manage and resolve production issues, minimise downtime, and implement preventative measures for future incidents. * Automation and Tool Development: Develop and maintain automation tools and scripts to streamline operations, reduce manual efforts, and increase system efficiency. Focus on automating routine tasks and deployment processes to enhance system stability. * Cross\-functional Collaboration: Work closely with development teams to integrate best practices and reliability into the software development lifecycle. Collaborate with product and support teams to understand customer needs and provide technical solutions. * Capacity Planning and Resource Optimization: Engage in capacity planning and resource optimization strategies to manage workload demands and resource utilisation, ensuring cost\-effective scalability and performance. * Mentoring Junior talent. * Participate in on\-call rota. #### **What skills do I need?** * At least five years production system administration/SRE experience. * At least three years serving a large\-scale SaaS web application solution with AWS, or similar cloud provider. * Strong experience with implementing infrastructure as code (e.g. CloudFormation, Terraform etc.), automation tooling (e.g. Puppet, Ansible etc.), CI/CD (e.g. Jenkins, Travis CI, GitLab etc.) * Experience in implementing observability tech stacks using tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Datadog, New Relic etc. * You are able to analyse and optimise performance in high\-traffic web applications. * Ability to solve complex, high\-impact problems. * Experience building and supporting large\-scale distributed systems that back a consumer app or website with associated requirements of performance, security and disaster recovery. * Able to effectively communicate technical ideas to and collaborate with both technical and non\-technical peers. * Experience deploying microservice environments, using containerisation technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes is an advantage. ***Don't worry if you don't tick every box in order to apply, we're always happy to review applications and take all experience into consideration. We do our best to provide feedback where we can!*** #### **Not required but considered a big plus** * Certification in AWS, any PaaS, and/or related technologies. #### **Why work with us?** * Competitive salary and company ESOP. * 25 days' annual leave and 1 annual wellness day. * Private health insurance and company pension. * Parental benefits, including up to 26 weeks' paid maternity leave, 4 weeks paid paternity leave, and coaching support for new parents. * Up to 4 weeks' per year working abroad (role eligibility applies). * Clear career progression opportunities — take LearnUpon where you think it can go. * A collaborative and supportive environment with regular team events. **What is the Hiring Process?** Our typical process generally works as follows: * Qualified applicants will be invited to schedule a screening call. * Successful candidates will then be invited to a series of practical interviews. * Finally, candidates will have a short interview with a member of our C\-Suite Team. * The successful candidate will be contacted with an offer to join our team. **LearnUpon is an Equal Opportunities Employer.** We do not discriminate on the basis of gender, marital status, family status, age disability, sexual orientation, race, religion, membership of the Traveller community, or any other legally protected status. By applying for this job, you agree to LearnUpon's Privacy Policy. Find out more about our privacy policy here Visit our Careers site to find out more about working for LearnUpon, and check us out on Instagram.

title: Senior Fitter / Fitter
description: **We're Hiring: Senior Fitter / Fitter (Maintenance Technician) \- Join a Global Automotive Leader!** ===================================================================================================== ### **Join the DECOTEK Automotive Team \- Driving Precision for Global Automotive Leaders** **DECOTEK Automotive**, based in the Irish midlands, employs a highly skilled workforce in a worldclass manufacturing environment. We design, develop, and manufacture premium exterior trim components for **top global automotive manufacturers** \- and we pride ourselves on supplying original parts directly to some of the biggest names in the industry. We are seeking highly skilled and motivated **Senior Fitters / Fitters (Maintenance Technicians)** to join our dynamic, innovative, and growing manufacturing environment. **This is a fulltime shift role. Candidates must be fully flexible to work a 3cycle shift pattern.** **Why Join DECOTEK Automotive?** -------------------------------- * Work with cutting\-edge manufacturing technology in a highly skilled environment * Be part of a company supplying world\-leading automotive OEMs * Join a culture that values safety, teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement * Grow your career with development, upskilling and crosstraining opportunities **Role Overview** ================= As a Senior Fitter / Fitter at DECOTEK, you'll play a key role in ensuring the optimal performance, reliability and safety of our equipment and production systems. You will support manufacturing, lead problem\-solving at the production cell, and drive continuous improvement for enhanced operational efficiency. **Key Responsibilities** ======================== ### **Maintenance \& Technical Expertise** * Ownership of maintenance and repair for plant machinery, tooling and mechanical systems * Troubleshoot mechanical/electrical/pneumatic/hydraulic issues with minimal downtime * Evaluate, repair and modify tools, mechanical assemblies, and production equipment * Plan and record all maintenance activities via the **PEMAC system** * Perform equipment calibrations, validations, and ensure compliance with CE/safety directives * Service equipment per manufacturer guidelines and technical specifications ### **Production \& Operational Support** * Provide consistent technical support to manufacturing to maximise OEE and output * Attend breakdowns, identify root causes, and action immediate corrective solutions * Lead and support new projects from commissioning to SOP * Participate in shift handovers and daily meetings to ensure seamless communication ### **Continuous Improvement \& Leadership** * Actively drive operational excellence, cost reduction, and efficiency improvements * Identify and resolve process\-related issues using strong diagnostic and analytical skills * Train, mentor and support junior technicians and apprentices * Collaborate crossfunctionally with engineering, production, suppliers and service teams ### **Safety \& Compliance** * Champion safe working practices, risk assessments and adherence to H\&S policies * Maintain all work areas within **6S methodology**: Sort, Set in Order, Shine, Standardise, Sustain \& Safety * Immediately report, escalate or isolate any unsafe equipment, guarding issues, or safety concerns **Skills \& Experience Required** ================================= * Level 6 qualification (or equivalent) in Mechanical Engineering or Maintenance Technology * Experience with complex engineering drawings, mechanical systems and diagnostic equipment * Strong troubleshooting skills with hands\-on experience in mechanical systems; exposure to electrical/pneumatic/hydraulic systems is a strong advantage * Experience with CMMS systems (SAP \& PEMAC preferred) * Excellent communication, organisational and problem\-solving abilities * Ability to work independently and as part of a high\-performing team * Flexibility to support shutdown periods, bank holidays, weekends when required * A strong commitment to personal development, upskilling, and continuous improvement **Ideal Candidates Will:** ========================== * Display leadership and initiative at all times * Embrace challenges and adapt to evolving technologies * Maintain exceptional attention to detail and quality * Thrive in a fast\-paced, technical environment * Want to grow and advance within a leading manufacturing organisation **Ready to Build the Future of Automotive Excellence?** ======================================================= If you're a skilled Fitter or Maintenance Technician looking for your next career step with a forward\-thinking automotive manufacturer, **we want to hear from you**. **Apply today and become part of the DECOTEK Automotive team.**

title: Senior Frontend Developer
description: **Senior Front\-End Engineer (React)** **Salary:** €80,000 to €120,000 DOE \+ benefits **Location:** Dublin City Centre (Hybrid \- 3 days per week in the office ) We’re working with a global financial services company building a modern, AI\-driven workflow platform. This is a **senior front\-end role focused purely on UI and user experience using React and TypeScript** . You’ll join a small innovation team and work closely with product and business stakeholders in Dublin. This is not a typical front\-end role. You’ll be shaping how the product looks and feels, not just building from specs. **What you’ll be doing** * **Build high\-quality UI using React and TypeScript** * Turn ideas into working prototypes quickly using AI tools * Work closely with product teams to shape user experience * Improve and influence UI decisions across the platform * Collaborate with backend teams for integration * Support junior engineers through code reviews **Must have** * 5\+ years of experience as software engineering * 3\+ years of strong experience with **React and TypeScript** * Proven track record **building user\-focused interfaces** * Comfortable using **AI tools to speed up development** * Able to prototype ideas quickly and iterate fast * Confident communicator who can challenge and influence decisions * Product mindset with a **strong sense of what good UX looks like** * Experience with **Docker** * Exposure to **AWS services and practices or modern architectures** * Background working before and after AI tools became mainstream **Why apply** * Work on a modern React and AI\-driven product * High impact role with real influence on product direction * Close collaboration with business and product teams * Long\-term project with strong investment and growth

title: Senior Hydrogeologist
description: **Purpose \& Scope of the Role** Join a well\-established and growing groundwater team within a wider geo\-environmental group of around 100 professionals operating across the UK and Ireland. This role supports the delivery of high\-profile groundwater frameworks and resource development projects, including work with water utilities and public sector clients. As a Consultant or Senior Hydrogeologist, you will focus on technical delivery, project management and the continued growth of groundwater services, contributing to both regional and national projects. **Your Impact in this Position** You will play a key role in developing sustainable groundwater resources, advising on feasibility, investigation, testing and implementation of new water supplies. Your expertise will directly influence abstraction licensing, environmental protection and water resource management. Alongside technical delivery, you will strengthen client relationships, support project delivery and contribute to the ongoing success and expansion of the groundwater business. ### **Core Capabilities** **Technical Delivery** * Deliver hydrogeological assessments from desk study through to reporting and implementation * Design and interpret pumping tests and groundwater investigations * Prepare abstraction licence applications and technical reports * Apply best practice and UK/NI regulatory guidance to groundwater projects **Project \& Commercial Management** * Manage project budgets, programmes and subcontractors * Support tendering, fee proposals and delivery of projects (£100k\+) * Maintain high standards of health, safety and quality across all work **Business Development \& Client Engagement** * Act as a key client contact, building strong working relationships * Support growth through frameworks and new opportunities * Contribute to client meetings, proposals and project delivery strategies **Fieldwork \& Site Leadership** * Plan and supervise field investigations, drilling and testing * Oversee water quality sampling and environmental assessments * Carry out site visits to support technical delivery and problem\-solving **Collaboration \& Mentoring** * Work within a multi\-disciplinary team across geo\-environmental services * Support and mentor junior team members * Contribute to technical excellence and knowledge sharing **Skills, Qualifications \& Experience** A degree in earth sciences, geology or geochemistry, with postgraduate qualification in hydrogeology. Strong experience in groundwater investigations, pumping test analysis and reporting is essential. You will have excellent communication skills, technical judgement and the ability to manage projects and client relationships. Chartered status and experience with modelling tools (e.g. MODFLOW) are advantageous. **Working Environment** This role can be based in Wexford or Belfast, with flexibility to work from other offices for experienced candidates. Projects span Great Britain, Ireland and occasionally international locations, with short periods of travel required. Hybrid and flexible working arrangements are supported, typically combining office, site and remote working. Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. We support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are *Leading with Science®* to solve our clients’ most complex problems. *In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role*

title: Senior Laboratory Operations Analyst (Senior QC Analyst)
description: **Position Overview** Helsinn Birex Pharmaceuticals is currently seeking a **Senior QC Analyst** to join our Laboratory Operations team. This role offers the opportunity to take a hands\-on leadership position within a dynamic and regulated environment. The successful candidate will be responsible for the supervision and development of QC Analysts, review and approval of analytical data, and coordination of laboratory schedules, ensuring compliance with cGMP and cGLP standards. This position plays a key role in supporting the site’s quality control operations and maintaining inspection readiness. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide leadership, training, and direction to QC Analysts and apprentices. * Develop and coordinate weekly work schedules and ensure effective task delegation. * Review analyst documentation to ensure accuracy and adherence to Right First Time KPIs. * Perform and oversee raw material, in\-process, and finished product testing (chemistry and microbiology). * Monitor trends and prepare monthly/annual reports for management. * Maintain and review laboratory SOPs, specifications, and training records. * Lead or support investigations, deviations, and change controls within the QC area. * Troubleshoot analytical issues and provide technical support to other departments. * Support audit preparation activities and participate in internal/external audits (HPRA/FDA). * Represent QC on cross\-functional projects and support equipment qualification/validation. **Required Qualifications and Experience** * BSc in a relevant scientific discipline (required). * 3–4 years of experience in a cGLP environment, ideally in HPRA/FDA\-approved facilities. * 1–2 years of experience in people management or team supervision in a laboratory setting (preferred). * Expertise in HPLC and GC methods (SME\-level preferred). * Strong data review and documentation skills with high attention to detail. * Demonstrated ability to manage workload and meet deadlines in a regulated environment. * Experience with regulatory audits and implementing corrective actions. * Proficiency with systems such as LIMS Sample Manager, Chromeleon, E09, and Trackwise Digital. **Key Competencies** * Strong leadership and mentoring abilities. * Excellent planning, organisational, and time management skills. * High level of technical and analytical problem\-solving capabilities. * Clear and professional communication skills, both verbal and written. * Ability to work collaboratively across departments. * Commitment to quality, continuous improvement, and compliance. * Proactive attitude with the ability to take initiative and make decisions. * Strong sense of accountability and ownership.

title: Senior Physicist
description: **About The Job** **Mercor** connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include **Benchmark** , **General Catalyst** , **Peter Thiel** , **Adam D'Angelo** , **Larry Summers** , and **Jack Dorsey** . **Position:** Physics Subject Matter Expert **Type:** **Contract** **Compensation:** **$85/hour** **Location:** **Remote** **Commitment:** **4–6 tasks/week** **Role Responsibilities** * Develop challenging problems in Physics to inform future AI innovations. * Create high\-quality data sets for AI model training and evaluation. * Collaborate with AI research teams to enhance model outputs. * Work independently and asynchronously to meet deadlines. * Leverage expertise in Statistical Mechanics, Condensed Matter, and AMO Physics. **Qualifications** **Must\-Have** * PhD in Physics with specialization in Advanced Quantum Mechanics, Advanced Electrodynamics, or Advanced Classical Mechanics. * Graduate degree from US/UK/Canada/Western Europe. * High attention to detail. * Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent proficiency in English. **Start Date** * February; applications reviewed on a rolling basis. **Application Process (Takes 20–30 mins to complete)** * Upload resume * AI interview based on your resume * Submit form **Resources \& Support** * For details about the interview process and platform information, please check: https://talent.docs.mercor.com/welcome/welcome * For any help or support, reach out to: support@mercor.com *PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.*

title: Senior Planning Manager
description: **Job Description** Reporting to the Operations Manager the planning Manager is responsible for leading project planning activities across major MEP projects in Ireland, Europe \& UK. The successful candidate will be responsible for developing, managing, and overseeing all project programmes, ensuring accurate progress reporting, critical path analysis, and effective communication with stakeholders. **Duties And Responsibilities** * Develop, maintain, and manage detailed project schedules using Primavera P6 and/or MS Project * Create baseline programmes and manage schedule updates * Conduct critical path analysis, resource loading, and forecasting * Identify programme risks and recommend mitigation strategies * Produce weekly and monthly progress reports * Support commercial teams with delay analysis and EoT submissions * Present programme updates to internal and external stakeholders * Collaborate with project, engineering, commercial, and site teams * Lead and mentor junior planners * Implement best‑practice planning processes and improve planning controls **Qualifications** **SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS** * 10\+ years planning experience in construction or MEP environments * Strong expertise in Primavera P6 (essential) * Experience delivering complex, high‑value projects * Strong understanding of project controls and risk management * Ability to interpret engineering drawings and specifications * Excellent communication and stakeholder‑management skills * Proven leadership and team‑management experience * Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or related field * PMI‑SP or similar certification beneficial * Experience in large‑scale MEP projects is desirable Additional Information Having an inclusive and flexible culture helps the business continue to grow as a strong, dynamic and innovative organisation. As Well As Being Supported And Encouraged To Develop Your Career Here At King And Moffatt, We Also Offer The Following * Competitive salary * Professional Development support * Employee Assistance Program * Health and Wellness programs At King and Moffatt, we believe in the power of diversity and inclusion to drive innovation, creativity, and success. We are committed to creating a workplace where all employees feel welcomed, valued, and respected regardless of their race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, disability, or any other dimension of diversity. We strive to build a culture that celebrates differences, encourages open dialogue, and promotes equal opportunities for growth and advancement. By embracing diversity and fostering an inclusive environment, we not only strengthen our organisation but also enrich the lives of our employees and the communities we serve

title: Senior Quantitative Risk Analyst, Risk Analytics, Dublin, Belfast, London, Northampton.
description: **Job Title: Senior Quantitative Risk Analyst – Risk Analytics, Level 3** **Location/Office Policy:** Dublin, Belfast, Northampton, London with Hybrid Working **What Is The Role** This role is positioned within the Risk Analytics Team as a **Senior Quantitative Risk Analyst.** In **Risk Analytics** , we develop and support the deployment of risk models, strategies and decision tools for regulatory capital, internal capital and business decision making. Risk Analytics is part of the Risk Function, this is an independent, second line of defence function that monitors, controls, and supports risk\-taking activities across AIB. The purpose of the Risk Function is to provide advice and guidance in relation to risk while providing independent oversight and reporting on AIB’s risk profile. The Risk Function’s main objective is to ensure AIB has a robust risk management framework and culture in place to ensure risks are taken within the risk appetite set by the Board, in support of AIB’s customer franchise and social responsibility. **Key Accountabilities.** * Analysis \& investigation: Undertake and guide junior data scientists in various complex data analyses, investigations and/or modelling of business issues to improve the management, services, and products of the bank. * Predictive model development: Take a leading role in building predictive models that are focussed on core business elements, such as automated decisioning, capital requirements and loss expectations. * Data insights: Perform and guide junior data scientists in exploratory and ad\-hoc data analysis with a view to generating insights and using this to deliver actionable recommendations to the Business. * Expert advice: Provide specialist advice to the business with an emphasis on the impact and application of risk management requirements. * Risk segmentation analysis: Creating segmentations that allow us to better understand the risks present in our lending portfolio and what we can do to better manage the risks. * Leadership: Mentoring and guidance for junior data scientists. Also, there will be responsibility for reviewing work carried out by junior team members. * Digital protection: Access / utilise bank data within the policies and frameworks required by AIB. **What you Will Bring;** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a model monitoring, model development or model validation role. Examples include IRB; IFRS 9; loss forecasting; stress testing or economic capital modelling; propensity modelling; or a combination thereof. * A bachelor’s degree in a quantitative analytical discipline (2\.1 or higher), e.g., mathematics, applied mathematics, physics, statistics, engineering, econometrics. (Confirmation will be sought if successful for the role.). * Ideally have advanced level of SAS or SQL programming – an equivalent level in an alternate programming language would be consider (e.g., R, Python, Matlab). Advanced experience in extracting, transforming, and cleaning data for modelling purposes. * Familiarity with data visualisation tools such as QlikView, Power BI, SAS VA or Tableau. * Experience writing technical documents that meet internal and regulatory standards. Experience in engagement with regulatory or audit bodies; * Strong ability to build relationships and communicate with key stakeholders, Curiosity, and inventiveness. * Curiosity and inventiveness. Good problem solving skills with capability to defend their decisions from challenge both on a technical and business front. **What We Offer** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. *Some of our benefits include;* * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year **As part of the selection process, the successful applicant will be expected to demonstrate the AIB Behaviours and ability in the Behavioural and Technical Capabilities reflected below** Please note that the capabilities will only be asked at interview stage. * Ensures Accountability * Collaborates * Develops \& Empowers * Data Analysis * Risk Modelling \& Scenario Analysis * Statistical Modelling If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the Recruiter for this role, Nicole Pasquetti, at careers@aib.ie for a conversation. **Application deadline: 1st April 2026**

title: Senior Recruitment Consultant - Irish Healthcare Market
description: **About the Business Division:** Since our inception in 2013, Remedium have championed the recruitment of permanent healthcare professionals, supporting our clients to drive more efficient and consistent patient\-led care, address future healthcare workforce staffing demands and reduce excessive agency locum spend. As the lead consultant in a new region, you’ll have full ownership of developing business relationships, building a sustainable recruitment desk, and driving revenue growth. Over time, as the market develops, there will be the opportunity to hire, lead, and manage a growing team, progressing into a leadership position within the division. **Key Responsibilities:** **Market Development \& Business Growth** * Independently build and lead the Irish Healthcare recruitment desk from the ground up. * Develop and implement strategic sales and marketing initiatives to increase market share and revenue. * Identify new business opportunities and establish strong, long\-term relationships with key clients including Trusts and the HSE and Private Hospitals. * Analyse market trends, competitor activity, and client needs to refine recruitment strategies. * Help identify Frameworks and Tenders to apply for. **360° Recruitment Delivery** * Manage the full recruitment lifecycle: sourcing, screening, interview coordination, offer management, and onboarding. * Business Development: Contact new Clients to build relationships and discuss job vacancies * Deliver exceptional service to both clients and candidates, ensuring long\-term partnerships. * Leverage digital platforms and social media to attract candidates and promote the brand. * Conduct on\-site visits and meetings to strengthen client relationships and gain a deep understanding of their needs. **Leadership \& Growth** * Operate with autonomy and take full accountability for results and outcomes. * Mentor and share best practices with junior colleagues as the team grows. * Play a key role in shaping the long\-term recruitment strategy for the Irish market. * Champion Remedium’s values and uphold the highest standards of professionalism and ethics. **Qualifications** **Skills \& Experience Required:** * Proven track record of success in healthcare recruitment (Irish market experience strongly required). * Strong business development and client relationship management skills. * Demonstrable ability to work independently and build new markets or desks from scratch. * Excellent communication, negotiation, and influencing skills, including with senior and C\-level stakeholders. * Resilient, proactive, and adaptable with a solutions\-oriented mindset. * Organised and able to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Commercially minded, target\-driven, and motivated by growth and success. * Educated to degree level or equivalent experience. **Why Join Us:** * Opportunity to own and grow a new market with significant autonomy and strategic input. * Clear career progression path into leadership and team management as the business scales. * Collaborative, values\-driven culture with strong support from senior leadership. * Competitive compensation structure, performance bonuses, and professional development opportunities. **Additional Information** ***About M3 EU:*** M3 EU is at the forefront of healthcare innovation, offering digital solutions across healthcare, life sciences, pharmaceuticals, and more. Since our inception in 2000, we’ve seen remarkable growth, fuelled by our mission to utilize the internet for a healthier world and more efficient healthcare systems. We provide a unique opportunity for you to be at the cutting edge of healthcare innovation, shaping the future in a meaningful career. Embrace the chance to drive change with M3 EU.

title: Senior Service & Support Engineer
description: **Overview:** The Senior Service \& Support Engineer plays a key role in delivering high\-quality technical support for AIR’s automation solutions. and mechanical systems. * Act as an escalation point for Support Engineers, assisting with complex or recurring issues. * Perform detailed root\-cause analysis and propose long\-term corrective actions. * Utilise advanced diagnostic tools and methods to resolve issues efficiently. **Customer \& Internal Stakeholder Interaction** * Engage directly with customers to resolve technical issues and provide clear communication of findings. * Translate complex concepts into accessible explanations for non\-technical stakeholders. * Contribute to high levels of customer satisfaction by ensuring timely and effective resolution of issues. **Collaboration \& Mentoring** * Work closely with internal SMEs (software, electrical, mechanical) to address escalated issues. * Share knowledge, best Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. * Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively across functions. * Customer\-focused mindset with high standards of service delivery. * Proactive, organised, and improvement oriented. **What we offer:** In AIR, we offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees' well\-being title: Senior Sonographer & Trainee Obstetrics Sonographer | Dún Laoghaire

Perform general ultrasound examinations independently over three days per week, producing high\-quality diagnostic images in accordance with Medica protocols and recognised best practices * Undertake two days per week of supervised obstetric ultrasound training, participating actively in clinical scanning, teaching sessions, and competency assessments * Deliver a patient\-centred service at all times, ensuring that every patient is treated with dignity, respect, efficiency, and confidentiality * Engage fully with the structured training programme, maintaining an up\-to\-date clinical portfolio and working towards achievement of agreed obstetric training milestones * Adhere to all Health and Safety policies, clinical governance frameworks, and incident reporting procedures to securely document all patient data using Medica’s Radiology Information System (RIS) and Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS), ensuring compliance with GDPR and data protection regulations * Attend in\-service training sessions, study days, and team meetings as required to support professional development and service improvement * Contribute to audit processes, quality assurance activities, and the development of protocols and procedures where appropriate * Promote a positive and professional image of Medica’s ultrasound service and uphold the organisation’s values in all interactions * Carry out any other duties reasonably required to support the effective delivery of general ultrasound services and successful completion of training We welcome people from all Our experience covers a wide range of projects in new and existing facilities and we offer a comprehensive range of services in Civil, Structural, Mechanical and Electrical Engineering, Architecture, Project Management and Quantity Surveying. McElroy Associates is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration. We are dedicated to providing a workplace free from discrimination and harassment, where everyone is treated with respect and has the opportunity to succeed. **Role Details:** **Location:** Dublin 02, travel to client’s premises may be required **Salary Range:** Depending on Experience, but starting between €38,000 \- €48,000 **Hours:** Monday to Thursday 08:30 – 17:15 pm, Friday 08:30 am – 16:00 pm **Responsibilities:** * Prepare detailed technical drawings and models using CAD software (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit, Civil 3D) * Assist engineers with the design of civil and structural elements * Produce drawings for planning, tender, and construction stages * Ensure all drawings comply with relevant standards and regulations * Coordinate with engineers, architects, and other stakeholders * Maintain accurate project documentation and drawing records * Support site teams with technical queries when required **Qualifications:** * Diploma in Civil/Structural Engineering * Typically 2–5 years’ preparing civil and/or structural drawings * Proficiency in AutoCAD and Revit, with the ability to produce detailed technical drawings to a high standard * Knowledge of relevant design standards and codes * Familiarity with BIM processes **Skills** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise workload and meet tight deadlines * Self\-motivated with excellent attention to detail * Problem\-solving mindset with a proactive approach * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team * Highly detail‑oriented with a strong commitment to producing high‑quality work. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €38,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Private medical insurance * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Talent Acquisition Co-ordinator
description: Vacancy Number: 28204 Category: Human Resources Job Type: Full\-time **Position Description** We are looking to recruit a Talent Acquisition Coordinator to meet the growing demands of the business within the Early Careers Talent Acquisition team. The successful applicant will work in the Talent Acquisition Centre of Excellence “CoE” and will be provided with the support and development needed to enable them to deliver successfully in the role. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage and monitor the advertising of resources in line with the Strategic Resource Plan and approval systems, working closely with HR Governance Specialists. * Coordinate and manage all candidate and applicant communications, interview scheduling, and support line managers and candidates throughout internal and external recruitment processes. * Maintain and update internal trackers and collate data to produce regular management, TA, and HR reports, including monthly recruitment metrics and KPIs. * Assist with Early Talent recruitment, including attending careers fairs, supporting graduate event coordination, and ensuring excellent relationships with all stakeholders. * Review and monitor employment contracts and documentation for external candidates and oversee the new hire process alongside the Onboarding Specialist to ensure compliance with legal and policy requirements. * Maintain the TA SharePoint site, respond to GDPR queries, action data clearance requests, and ensure ongoing compliance with data protection regulations. * Champion continuous improvement initiatives with TA Team Leaders, stay updated on recruitment market trends and employment legislation, and demonstrate flexibility and adaptability to change. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * At least 1\-2 years of relevant work experience. * Ability to meet tight deadlines with accuracy and attention to detail. * Knowledge of HR practices, policies, procedures, and employment legislation. * Proficiency in MS Office, especially advanced Excel, and familiarity with SharePoint. * Strong communication, interpersonal, planning, and organisational skills. * Flexible approach to working. * Ability to work effectively within a team. **Desirable** * A HR qualification or equivalent relevant work experience would be an advantage. * SharePoint and SAP knowledge. **Location** This position will be based in 27 Fitzwilliam Street Lower, Dublin 2\. *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** Early Careers Team Lead **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €33,500\- €40,000 per annum depending on experience **Closing Date** 2nd April 2026 *Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role.* **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Tax Graduate Programme 2026
description: **Join Crowe Ireland’s 2026 Tax Graduate Programme!** Are you ready to kickstart your career and write your story with one of Ireland's top accounting and advisory firms? At **Crowe Ireland** , we’re not just about numbers—we’re about people, innovation, and making a real impact. We’re advisors, partners, and innovators who help businesses grow, navigate their challenges, and build a brighter future. We work with a diverse mix of clients – from owner\-managed business and not\-for\-profit to multinationals, government agencies and hospitality as a small example – Every day brings something new! As part of our team, you'll learn from the best, gain hands\-on experience across a range of industries, and be part of a community that values **collaboration** , **curiosity** , and **creativity** . **Why choose Crowe Ireland?** At Crowe Ireland, we are a close\-knit team with a global reach. Our firm is all about fostering talent, encouraging fresh ideas, and driving positive change for our clients and community. We are big enough to offer great opportunities, international assignments and write a successful story and small enough for you to make great impacts on some of our projects from the word go. Join us, and you will be part of a dynamic and diverse team that values growth, learning and having a bit of fun along the way! If you want to know more about our Graduate Programme, visit our website. **What’s in it for You?** Our 2026 Tax Graduate Programme is designed to give you real\-world experience, professional development, and a fast track to becoming a qualified accountant and/or tax advisor. You’ll get to work with amazing clients, use trending technology, get a flavour of all specialties in Tax and learn from a team that genuinely cares about your success. Here’s what you’ll do: **Your Role:** 1. **Explore a Range of Tax Disciplines** : Get hands\-on experience with different areas of tax, including International Tax, Corporate Tax, Global Mobility, Personal Taxation and VAT. You’ll help businesses navigate the complexities of tax laws both locally and globally. 2. **Build leadership skills** : You will be assigned a portfolio of clients to manage. Managers and Partners will coach and support you, also review the quality of the service provided, but you will have the opportunity to build autonomy and leadership skills from day 1\. 3. **Become a Data Detective** : Use the latest tools and techniques to analyse client data, research tax technical issues, and find smart solutions that add real value to our clients. 4. **Build Meaningful Relationships** : You will work closely with clients and collaborate with other departments to deliver comprehensive solutions that meet their needs. 5. **Communication Pro** : Ensure managers, directors, and partners are always informed of progress, potential issues, and key insights on your assignments. You will work closely with partners, directors and other managers to deliver top\-notch services to our clients. 6. **Deliver Excellence** : Contribute to high\-quality tax assignments, ensuring compliance, mitigating risks, and driving positive outcomes for our clients. **How we are going to support your development:** * Crowe will fully sponsor your accounting qualification: You will have the freedom to choose which accounting qualification you will pursue. ACA? ACCA? It’s your pick! * In addition to your accounting qualification, we will also sponsor a Chartered Tax Advisor (CTA) qualification for you. * We will support your studies by providing generous study leave so you can dedicate time to pass your exams. * Flexibility to attend your classes and tuitions. * You will work closely with our senior team and will be mentored and supported to achieve your personal and professional goals. * You will gain experience and develop competencies in practice dealing with clients and carrying out meaningful and relevant work **What We’re Looking For:** * **Academic Performance** : You achieved or are on track to achieve a 2\.2 or higher in your undergraduate degree and have recently graduated or will graduate in 2026\. * **Future Accountants:** You’re excited about a career in Tax and accounting, no matter your background. * **Eligibility:** You have the right to work in the Republic of Ireland. * **Communication Champs:** You’ve got strong verbal and written communication skills, with an eye for detail and a knack for problem\-solving. * **Attention to Detail:** You’re a master of the small stuff and know that details matter. **Our offer:** * Alongside a fully sponsored accounting qualification, we offer a competitive reward package that comprises salary, benefits, development opportunities and wellbeing programmes. Some of them are: * 22 days of annual leave * Pension with 100% employer matching contributions * Tax saver travel pass * Cycle to Work Scheme * And more… * Work\-life balance: our team enjoy compressed working weeks during the summer and hybrid work options. * Accelerate career progression through ongoing support and investment in your development. **Why Join Us?** Because we believe in nurturing talent from day one. You will get mentorship from experienced professionals, access to ongoing learning and development opportunities, and the chance to make a real impact in a supportive, inclusive environment. Plus, we know how to have fun—whether it is team social events, community events, or just celebrating a job well done! Ready to write a story that matters? Apply now and start your journey with Crowe! **Equal opportunity** Crowe is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and anyone seeking employment at Crowe is considered based on merit, qualifications, competence and talent. We don’t regard colour, religion, race, national origin, sexual orientation, ancestry, citizenship, sex, marital or family status, disability, gender, or any other legally protected status when making our hiring decisions. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

title: Technology Transfer Manager
description: **Technology Transfer Manager** **About Arcadis** Arcadis is the world’s leading company in sustainable design, engineering, and consultancy for natural and built assets. With over 36,000 professionals across 70\+ countries, we are united in our mission to improve quality of life and solve the world’s most complex challenges. **Be part of something meaningful – join us!** **Technology Transfer Manager** **Objective:** The Associate Director Manufacturing Sciences Management is responsible for overseeing teams and processes involved in converting raw materials and components into finished goods. This role ensures the implementation of strategies that align with long\-term business objectives and global corporate goals. By leading multiple teams and ensuring appropriate talent selection, this role contributes to the success of manufacturing operations. **Responsibilities** * Lead and manage multiple teams involved in supporting and developing manufacturing processes (PhD chemists, Process Validation and Data Science) * Lead a team PhD Chemists and development laboratory to enhance manufacturing efficiency and quality * Establish and implement long\-term strategies for manufacturing operations * Ensure successful Technical Transfer of new products to the Grange Castle site. Collaborate effectively with Global Teams (Pharmaceutical Sciences and Manufacturing Sciences) * Solve complex problems in manufacturing settings, providing innovative solutions * Work with Head of Finance to identify annual opportunities for R\&D Tax credits * Ensure compliance with Global Validation standards * Enhance and innovate Data Science – develop use of computational modelling to ensure right first time technical transfer. Develop use of AI and other tools to predict * Process Validation \- lead and develop team of validation SMEs, ensure compliance with regulatory standards and maintain validated state of site manufacturing processes **Education** * PhD degree in a scientific discipline * \>10 years industry experience * Desired minimum years management experience * Demonstrates strong leadership skills, capable of guiding and inspiring teams * Experienced in managing teams, fostering collaboration and effective delegation * Utilizes continuous improvement methodologies to enhance manufacturing efficiency and quality * Ensures rigorous quality assurance and integrates safety protocols to uphold standards * Proficient in manufacturing engineering principles and practices * Communicates effectively with peers and customers, ensuring clear and consistent messaging * Solves complex problems in manufacturing settings, providing innovative solutions Any more questions feel free to contact Shane Whyte on 014661724 or alternatively shane.whyte@arcadis.com **Why Join Arcadis?** At Arcadis, we empower individuals to excel because your contributions matter. Taking a skills\-based approach, we provide opportunities for you to shape your career, maximize your impact, and grow professionally. By joining us, you’ll engage in meaningful work that drives sustainable solutions for a better planet while leaving a lasting legacy. **Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion** We are dedicated to creating an environment where everyone can thrive. Through our six ED\&I workstreams—Age, Disability, Faith, Gender, LGBT\+, and Race—we foster innovation by bringing together diverse perspectives. Together, we strive to improve quality of life while building a more inclusive future **Join Arcadis. Create a legacy**

title: Trainee Accountant
description: **Trainee Accountant** **Dublin \| Full\-Time \| Practice** A busy and well\-regarded accountancy practice is looking to hire a Trainee Accountant to join its growing team. This opportunity will suit someone with 1–2 years’ experience in practice who is keen to build on their existing foundation, gain strong hands\-on exposure, and continue progressing their accountancy career in a supportive environment. This is a good role for someone who wants broad exposure across accounts preparation, Month \-end, client interaction and day\-to\-day finance work, while working closely with more senior members of the team. **Key responsibilities:** * Assisting with the preparation of year\-end accounts for a range of clients * Supporting the preparation of management accounts * Assisting with VAT, PAYE and Revenue filings * Performing bank reconciliations and balance sheet reconciliations * Processing journals and maintaining accurate financial records * Assisting with bookkeeping duties for a portfolio of clients * Supporting senior team members with audit and accounts assignments where required * Liaising with clients to gather financial information and resolve queries * Assisting with general practice administration and ad hoc finance duties as they arise **Requirements:** * 1–2 years’ experience working in an accountancy practice * Exposure to accounts preparation, bookkeeping and VAT work * Currently studying towards ACCA, ACA, CIMA or interested in continuing professional exams * Strong attention to detail and a good work ethic * Good communication skills and a professional manner with clients * Strong Excel skills and good systems awareness * Eager to learn, develop and take direction from senior staff * Must hold an EU / Irish Passport or stamp 4 visa **The ideal person:** You will have already gained a solid grounding in practice and now want to join a team where you can continue learning and take on broader responsibilities. You will be organised, reliable and keen to develop your technical skills in a structured but hands\-on environment.

description: **Role Overview** NBC Global Finance Limited (“NBCGF”) is currently offering a traineeship opportunity for the 2027/28 training cycle. The successful applicant will join a dynamic Legal Team of four senior solicitors and will be given the opportunity to develop foundational inhouse legal skills within a regulated financial services environment. The position will align with the Professional Practice Course (PPC) schedule (fulltime) and the 24month in office training period and will provide support to key legal workstreams, including contract negotiation, advisory and legal governance, while meeting the Law Society of Ireland training requirements. * Reports to: General Counsel (or delegated supervising solicitor). * Location: Ballsbridge, Dublin. * Contract: Fixed term traineeship aligned to Law Society of Ireland requirements. **What to Expect as part of a Traineeship with NBCGF?** The traineeship opportunity with NBCGF will offer trainees valuable exposure to financial services, specifically in the area of capital markets and the trainee can expect exposure to a variety of workstreams, including: * Contract negotiation of both trading and vendor agreements; * Maintaining and contributing to legal precedents; * Performance of legal research; * Communicating effectively with a variety of internal stakeholders as required; * Providing generalized support to the Legal Team through performance of ad\-hoc duties across the activities of the entire company e.g. employment law, transactions matters, property issues etc; and * Hands\-on contribution to NBCGF’s wider strategic initiatives. **General knowledge, skills and experience required** We are seeking applicants with: * Strong academic performance in their Leaving Certificate or equivalent; * 2:1 or higher in their undergraduate degree (any discipline welcome). * All FE 1 examinations completed; * Law firm placement experience (desirable but not essential); * Clear and concise verbal and written communication skills with attention to detail; * Proficiency in use of Microsoft 365, including PowerPoint, Excel and Word. More sophisticated technology skills are beneficial; * Ability to demonstrate strong prioritization and time management skills as well as seeing tasks through to completion; * Ability to work effectively as part of a larger team and interact effectively with key stakeholders as well as the ability to demonstrate ownership of tasks where required; and * Willingness to learn and develop new skills. **Other Desired Skills and Experience** * Exposure to the principles of commercial contract negotiation; and * Interest in financial markets/products. **Application Process** For more information or to apply for this position, please submit a cover letter and CV to hradmin@nbcgf.ie . The selection process will consist of three rounds; two competency\-based interviews and a brief presentation to a three\-person panel. Further information on NBCGF can be found on our website: www.nbcgf.ie . **The Use of AI** We understand that AI can be a useful tool for application and interview preparation. While we encourage you to embrace such technology, it’s essential that your application and interview responses reflect your authentic self and are aligned with NBCGFs’ values. Your personal contributions are what will ultimately set you apart. **About Us** NBCGF is an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of National Bank of Canada (“NBC”). The main strategic purpose of NBCGF is to enable the NBC Group (“NBC Group,” meaning National Bank of Canada and all its subsidiaries) to grow and diversify its presence in Europe while allowing for a natural development of its existing business relationships and the establishment of new ones in the European markets. NBC has over 150 years of history and is currently the 6th largest Canadian bank. Through its branch and subsidiary network, NBC can service clients throughout North America and Europe. NBC’s strategy is to further develop NBCGF as its hub for trading and financial services in Europe. **What NBCGF has to Offer:** Access to competitive working conditions, market salary, wide range of employee benefits, and a dynamic work environment Open, respectful workplace where all employees can achieve their full potential. NBCGF recognises that the culture of an organisation and the performance of its workforce are not mutually exclusive. Since our inception in 2013, NBCGF has worked tirelessly to ensure our cultural framework aligns not only with the Code of Conduct and Ethics of our parent company, NBC, but with our own environmental and local values. Promoting and supporting diversity in the workplace is an important aspect of good people’s management\- it’s about valuing everyone in the organisation as an individual. We at NBCGF have an effective diversity and inclusion approach concerning our employees. There are no silos in our teams and management strive to add value to our organisation, by contributing to employee well\-being and engagement. NBCGF takes pride in its diverse work force, employing and cultivating professionals from all over the globe. Working at NBCGF can take different forms and it is built around the relationship of trust between the employer and its employees. NBCGF operates a hybrid working model where the employee’s time can be split between the office and a remote location on a schedule which may vary. The model considers customer expectations, organisational needs, and constraints, as well as the employees’ need for flexibility and the nature of the work they perform. What is important is having the right arrangements in place so that you can best perform your role. We're putting people first. We're a bank on a human scale that stands out for its courage, entrepreneurial culture, and passion for people. Our mission is to have a positive impact on people’s lives. We want our recruitment process to be fully accessible. If you require accommodation, feel free to let us know during your first conversations with us. We welcome all candidates! What can you bring to our team? *All data received from applicants is subject to GDPR and Data Protection requirements. Please see link to NBCGF website for NBCGF External Privacy Notice .*

title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (SURGERY)
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (SURGERY)** **Department: UCD Beacon Hospital Academy \& UCD School of Medicine** **Reports to:** **Administrative \& Clinical Tutor Responsibilities:** Dr Ruth Pilkington, Director of UCD Beacon Academy, Beacon Hospital **Date: July 2026 (Changeover)** **Available as Full\-time (Tutor \+ Clinical) or Part\-time (Tutor only)** **Overall Purpose of Job** Beacon Hospital has an academic partnership with University College Dublin and one of our principle objectives within this partnership is to support the clinical education programme in the UCD School of Medicine. The clinical tutor role plays an instrumental role in identifying key teaching moments for our undergraduate students who are attached to specialities as part of their experiential learning. All clinical tutors are part of a vibrant community of academic staff who pass on their clinical experience and expertise to the next generation of medical professionals. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables: Clinical Tutor (Surgery)** 1\.1\. Coordinate and participate in the teaching and training of the students in Beacon Hospital. 1\.1\.1\. Provide tutorial support and facilitate shared learning experiences for students while on placements. 1\.1\.2\. Provide lectures as part of the formal clinical curriculum and assist with the preparation of clinical examinations. 1\.1\.3\. Provide a level of supervision of clinical activity appropriate to the competence and experience of the individual student. 1\.1\.4\. Utilise a range of appropriate methods of teaching and assessment to demonstrate clinical learning opportunities \- i.e. OSCE style tutorials, problem\-based learning and bed\-side tutorials. 1\.2\. Liaise with relevant UCD personnel to ensure that clinical placements satisfy academic requirements and ensure integrated delivery of all courses. 1\.3\. Coordinate and partake in the orientation and appraisal of medical students attending Beacon Hospital. 1\.4\. Partake in the competency assessment of interns using the national intern competency framework tools within 30 working days of the intern's commencement date. 1\.5\. Demonstrate leadership by assisting in the professional and personal development of the students and Interns throughout their clinical placement at the Beacon Hospital through promotion of self\-learning, formal lectures and tutorials. 1\.6\. Act as a resource for students and Interns seeking specialty information and guidance. 1\.7\. Ensure that the relevant information about progress and performance is made available to the Beacon Hospital and UCD student placement coordinator. 1\.8\. Act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of the UCD Beacon Hospital Academy. 1\.9\. Adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI standards, Code Blue, Infection Prevention and Control, Falls Reduction and data protection. 1\.10\. Contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall clinical education is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. 1\.11\. Build upon the library of tutorial content available in the hospital. The tutor is required to prepare tutorials that can be used in each of the module s \- i.e. Surgery A/B and Clin Res 1, and that can be reproduced with ease each year by clinical tutors at the Beacon Hospital. These may include high level overviews of certain topics that can be taught to students with supporting artefacts, media, and learning tools. 1\.12\. Engage actively in research and scholarly activity as appropriate to the school or hospital. 1\.13\. Champion the student and Interns' participation in clinical audits to ensure that UCD Beacon Hospital Academy programmes meet quality and patient safety standards and any other applicable performance measures. 1\.14\. Report to the appointed clinical lead consultant within the Beacon in all matters relating to medical students and Interns in the manner requested by said clinical lead. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Current relevant Full Irish Medical Council Registration. MRCS/Membership of relevant college is desirable **Experience** Minimum 3 years postgraduate experience in an acute hospital setting. Experience in clinical education of students and junior staff. Experience in the effective management of a poor performing staff member and or student. Attendance at clinical tutor trainee days is required (if not complete the successful candidate will be given the opportunity to attend.) **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** High\-level interpersonal and communication skills (both oral and written) facilitating effective interaction within the work areas, the university and other institutions is a mandatory requirement. Strong organisational skills, ability to prioritise work, to work to deadlines and to use own initiative is a mandatory requirement. Proven experience of working in a quality assurance/improvement environment is a mandatory requirement. Proven ability to work as part of a team is required. Ability to embrace change in a dynamic environment, willingness to adopt new procedures and undertake further appropriate training as required Experience in teaching and examining at third level is desirable. Experience in curriculum development and the development of clinical education programmes is desirable. Evidence of research and peer reviewed publications is desirable. Good knowledge of university rules, regulations and procedures, especially those pertaining to examinations, registration and student welfare is required. Experience in research development at third level is desirable. Experience of general and academic administration is desirable. Display awareness and appreciation of the service users and the ability to empathise with and treat others with dignity and respect. Demonstrate sufficient clinical knowledge and evidence\-based practice to carry the out duties and responsibilities of the role. Demonstrate an ability to apply knowledge to best practice standards. All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**
